event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins with a black screen and melancholic acoustic guitar music, introducing Cristiano Ronaldo's early career with a deep, accented male narrator stating, "With a huge talent and big ambitions, that's how Cristiano Ronaldo started his career." Stylized montages depict his youth: a black-and-white photo in an 'AQUIMAR' jersey against a family portrait and a church on a rocky hill, followed by a sepia-toned image of a teenage Cristiano in a green-and-white striped jersey with action shots and a grand architectural background. The narrator emphasizes that "talent alone is never enough," highlighting "hard work, strong will, self-confidence, and a strong personality." A red-tinted image of Cristiano in a Manchester United jersey celebrating appears, juxtaposed with a split-screen of a young Cristiano with a whistle and a mature Cristiano holding a large silver trophy. The narrator concludes, "This is the story of Cristiano's beginnings and how he got to the pinnacle of football." Vibrant footage shows a young Cristiano in a blue training environment, then dynamic clips of him playing in a red Manchester United jersey, dribbling and scoring, accompanied by excited commentator exclamations like, "Oh my goodness, what a goal! And he scores the goal!" and "Ronaldo!" The segment opens with the glowing text 'MADEIRA 1985 - 1997' on a black screen, accompanied by the same acoustic music, as the narrator details Cristiano's first steps in Funchal, Madeira. Vintage photographs show him as a baby with parents, then as a young boy in a white suit in a family portrait, with a white church on a rocky hill in the background. The narrator explains Cristiano's early focus on football over studies and his mother's support due to the family's poor background. His early football journey is shown via a close-up of his regional football license from 'Clube Futebol Andorinha' (1992-1995), followed by a youth team photo in blue jerseys. His father worked as a kitman at Andorinha. Fernao de Sousa, Cristiano's godfather, is interviewed outdoors, revealing that news of young Cristiano reached Fernando Soze, the youth coach of Nacional and Ronaldo's godfather, who significantly influenced his early career decisions, especially with his father Denis's struggles with alcohol. A photo of a teenage Cristiano with his father is shown, followed by the dark logo of Marítimo Madeira and a monochrome shield logo, likely Nacional. The narrator mentions Marítimo's attempts to sign Ronaldo. A vintage still image shows a young boy in a black-and-white 'AQUIMADEIRA' jersey beside a monochrome Club Logo (Shield with Stripes). An animated graphic explains that "two sets of kits and 20 footballs was the sealer to get Cristiano, who was only 10 years old." Grainy archival footage depicts the boy playing on a dirt pitch in the same kit. At Nacional, Cristiano experienced his "first taste of a real football academy," where coaches noted his "undernourishment" and advised his mother to provide a diet of "plenty of fish and meat," acknowledging his inherent talent. A Youth Football Team Photo shows him in 'EUROPA' jerseys. Dynamic match highlights follow, showing a player scoring and celebrating, then the boy running in a white t-shirt and blue shorts. The narrator stresses Cristiano's dedication, training "10 to 12 hours daily" with the ball due to having "no school, homework, and no house chores," leading him to become "too big for the island" within years. The scene shifts to João de Freitas, in a suit, pointing at a Poster with a golden Ballon d'Or Trophy. The narrator explains Cristiano's godfather again mentioned Ronaldo's name to João de Freitas, president of the Sporting Fan Club in Madeira. João de Freitas is then seen walking along a Coastal Waterfront Setting with a view of the coastal town and the Church on Rocky Hill, reinforcing the Madeira setting, before the segment concludes with a wide cityscape shot, all underscored by dramatic background music. The video culminates with Cristiano in a suit holding the golden Ballon d'Or trophy, a title card displaying 'balón' in red, and the text 'CRISTIANO ASCENDING TO GREATNESS' on a black background as the music fades.

Title Card 'balón'.description: A bold red text on a dark background, likely the name of the documentary or series, with a stylized font.

Family Portrait.description: A vintage photo showing Cristiano’s parents and a younger sibling, with the mother in a red-and-white striped dress and the father in a patterned shirt, standing outdoors near a stone monument. The narrator mentions the family's 'relatively poor background' and his mother's agreement to his football dreams.

Church on Rocky Hill.description: Visible in the background of the coastal cityscape, a white church perched on a rocky outcrop, anchoring the setting in Madeira and providing geographical context.

Grand Architectural Background.description: A classical building with columns and statues, used as a backdrop in the teenage Ronaldo montage, suggesting prestige and legacy.

Cristiano Ronaldo.description: Appears as a baby being held by his parents, then as a young boy in a white suit in a family portrait, and later as a teenager in a blue football jersey with his youth team. The narrator details his early life in Madeira, his focus on football over school due to his family's poor background, his mother's support for his dreams, his time playing for local amateur club Andorinha from 1992 to 1995, and being the subject of interest from Nacional and Marítimo.

Title Card 'CRISTIANO ASCENDING TO GREAT.description: White, glowing text on a black background, serving as the main title of the video segment.

Ballon d'Or Trophy.description: A golden, soccer-ball-shaped trophy depicted on a poster, held by a man in a suit, symbolizing the pinnacle of football achievement and connecting the past to present-day recognition.

Manchester United Jersey.description: A red long-sleeved jersey with 'vodafone' and 'AIG' sponsorship logos, worn during live-action gameplay and celebrations, specifically during a goal-scoring moment accompanied by excited commentator shouts.

Club Logo (Shield with Stripes).description: A monochrome shield with vertical black-and-white stripes and a central emblem, displayed prominently in the opening graphic, symbolizing a local football club from Cristiano’s youth.

Regional Football License.description: A close-up of Cristiano’s official license from 'Associação de Futebol do Funchal' for 'Clube Futebol Andorinha', with his name, photo, and license number '17.182'. The narrator confirms he played for Andorinha from 1992 to 1995.

Youth Football Team Photo.description: A group photo of young boys in black-and-white jerseys with 'EUROPA' on the front, crouching in the front row, including the young boy who is the focus of the narrative.

Fernao de Sousa.description: Identified visually as Cristiano’s godfather in a present-day interview, wearing a light blue shirt, speaking outdoors with a sports facility in the background. The narrator refers to him as 'Fernando Soze,' Ronaldo's godfather, and states he played a 'big role in Cristiano's early career decisions.'

Title Card 'MADEIRA 1985 - 1997'.description: A glowing, pixelated text on a black background, introducing the time period and location of the narrative, which the narrator confirms as Cristiano's early life in Madeira and Funchal.

Marítimo Madeira Logo.description: A dark, stylized emblem featuring a white lion within a ship’s wheel, with 'Marítimo' arched above and 'Madeira' below. The narrator identifies it as 'the biggest club in Madeira, Marítimo,' which attempted to sign Ronaldo.

João de Freitas.description: A man in a suit, identified by on-screen text and the narrator as 'João de Freitas, President of the Sporting Fan Club in Madeira,' seen pointing at a poster of the Ballon d'Or Trophy and walking along a waterfront, representing the local football community’s connection to the narrative.

Coastal Waterfront Setting.description: A scenic view of a coastal town with a harbor, buildings, and a hilltop church, serving as the backdrop for the present-day scenes with João de Freitas.

Poster with Ballon d'Or Trophy.description: A large outdoor poster featuring the golden Ballon d'Or Trophy, with text in Portuguese promoting a local event or sponsorship, used as a visual link to football excellence.

Archival Football Match Footage.description: Grainy, low-resolution video clips of youth football games on a dirt pitch, showing the young boy playing in black-and-white and white-blue kits, visually representing Cristiano's early football experiences and his intense training regimen of '10 to 12 hours daily' as described by the narrator, despite concerns about his 'undernourishment' from coaches at Nacional.

raw_segments: The video opens on a black screen, accompanied by gentle, melancholic acoustic guitar music. As the music continues, a male narrator with a deep, slightly accented voice introduces the segment, stating, "With a huge talent and big ambitions, that's how Cristiano Ronaldo started his career." This narration accompanies a stylized montage of Cristiano's early life, beginning with a black-and-white photo of a young Cristiano in an 'AQUIMAR' jersey, set against a family portrait and a scenic church on a rocky hill. The visual transitions to a sepia-toned image of a teenage Cristiano in a green-and-white striped jersey, with a younger version and an action shot beside him, all against a grand architectural background. The narrator continues, emphasizing that "talent alone is never enough," and lists "hard work, strong will, self-confidence, and a strong personality" as key factors. During this, a red-tinted image of Cristiano in a Manchester United jersey celebrating appears, followed by a split-screen showing a young Cristiano with a whistle and a mature Cristiano holding a large silver trophy. The narrator concludes, "This is the story of Cristiano's beginnings and how he got to the pinnacle of football." The montage then shifts to vibrant real-action footage, showing a close-up of a young Cristiano in a blue training environment, followed by dynamic clips of him playing in a red Manchester United jersey, dribbling, and scoring a goal. As he scores and celebrates with teammates, an excited commentator exclaims, "Oh my goodness, what a goal! And he scores the goal!" followed by another commentator shouting, "Ronaldo!" The video culminates with Cristiano in a suit holding the golden Ballon d'Or trophy, a title card displaying 'balón' in red, and finally, the bold white text 'CRISTIANO ASCENDING TO GREATNESS' on a black background, as the acoustic guitar music fades out.

The segment opens with a black screen displaying the glowing, pixelated text 'MADEIRA 1985 - 1997', setting a nostalgic tone, accompanied by gentle, melancholic acoustic guitar music. A male narrator begins, stating that on the islands of Madeira, particularly in Funchal, Cristiano took his first steps towards a footballing career. Vintage photographs of Cristiano Ronaldo’s early life appear, showing him as a baby held by his parents, then as a young boy in a white suit in a family portrait with his mother in a red-and-white striped dress and father in a patterned shirt, with a white church on a rocky hill in the background. The narrator explains that Cristiano focused very early on football instead of studying, and due to his family's relatively poor background, his mother supported his dreams. The visual narrative then shifts to his early football journey, showing a close-up of his regional football license from 'Clube Futebol Andorinha' with his name and the years '92-95' highlighted. A team photo follows, showing a young Cristiano in a blue jersey seated in the front row with his youth team. The narrator confirms Cristiano played for Andorinha from 1992 to 1995, where his father worked as a kitman. An interview with Fernao de Sousa, identified as Cristiano’s godfather, appears in a sunlit outdoor setting. The narrator reveals that news of young Cristiano reached Fernando Soze, the youth coach of Nacional and Ronaldo's godfather, who played a significant role in his early career decisions, especially given the absence of his father Denis, who 'drank away his sorrows'. A photo of a teenage Cristiano with his father is shown, followed by the dark, stylized logo of Marítimo Madeira, and then a monochrome shield logo, likely representing Nacional. The narrator concludes by mentioning that despite attempts by Marítimo, the biggest club in Madeira, to sign Ronaldo, Nacional...

The video opens with a vintage-style still image of a young boy in a black-and-white 'AQUIMADEIRA' football jersey, standing beside a monochrome Club Logo (Shield with Stripes). An animated graphic appears, illustrating that "two sets of kits and 20 footballs was the sealer to get Cristiano, who was only 10 years old," as the narrator explains his early value. This transitions to grainy, archival footage of youth football matches, showing the boy actively running and playing on a dirt pitch in the same black-and-white kit. The narrator details how "at Nacional, Cristiano had his first taste of a real football academy," where coaches were "worried because of Cristiano's undernourishment" and asked his mother to provide a diet of "plenty of fish and meat," though "the talent was there." The boy is then seen in a Youth Football Team Photo, crouching in the front row with teammates in 'EUROPA' jerseys. The footage continues with dynamic match highlights, showing a player scoring and celebrating, followed by the boy running in a white t-shirt and blue shorts. The narrator emphasizes Cristiano's dedication, stating that "whereas a typical kid at his age would train for two hours a day, Cristiano used to train with the ball between 10 to 12 hours daily" due to having "no school, homework, and no house chores to worry about." This intense training meant that "within a couple of years, Cristiano became too big for the island," and his "next step was a matter of time." The scene shifts to a present-day setting, showing João de Freitas, a man in a suit, pointing at a Poster with a golden Ballon d'Or Trophy. The narrator reveals that "Cristiano's godfather played a big role again as he mentioned Ronaldo's name to João de Freitas, who was the president of the Sporting Fan Club in Madeira." João de Freitas is then seen walking along a Coastal Waterfront Setting with a scenic view of a coastal town and the Church on Rocky Hill, reinforcing the Madeira setting, before the segment ends with a wide shot of the cityscape. Throughout, a consistent, slightly dramatic background music plays.

event_blocks.summary: A title card introduces Aurelio Pereira, Chief Scout of Sporting CP, as a dramatic instrumental track begins, with the narrator identifying him as the scout responsible for discovering talents like Futre, Figo, Quaresma, Nani, and Moutinho, whose images flash in a collage. Pereira, an older man in a green sweater, walks past a lion statue, symbolizing Sporting's emblem, as the narrator explains his scouting was based on intuition, leading him to give Cristiano a chance. A split-screen graphic displays the logos of Nacional and Sporting CP with a '€25000' transfer fee, showing two young boys in respective team jerseys beneath it, clarifying Nacional owed Sporting this amount and offered Cristiano to cover the debt. Pereira walks across a sunlit football pitch, his feet stepping on a white line, as the narrator describes Cristiano flying alone from Madeira to Lisbon. A panoramic Lisbon cityscape is overlaid with faded youth football match footage where a young player in a green-and-white jersey dribbles past an opponent, with 'VULCANO' visible on a banner. The narrator concludes that Aurelio and the coaches were struck by Cristiano's personality, not just his talent, as Pereira is shown sitting in a dugout. The segment opens with a close-up of Pereira in a dugout, as the narrator explains he agreed to Cristiano's permanent stay at Sporting's Academy in the summer of '97. A split-screen shows Pereira and a young boy, likely Cristiano, eating a snack, followed by a title card 'SPORTING 1997 - 2003' and shots of Lisbon. The narrator details Cristiano's 'huge difficulties' at the academy, including his accent, placement in an older age group, and living far from family, while visuals show him stretching, standing behind a goal, and training. Despite the ease of returning to Madeira, Cristiano 'stayed in the capital Lisbon to achieve his dreams,' as archival footage shows him sweeping a street. The segment concludes with a youth football team photo featuring a determined young Cristiano Ronaldo, while the narrator describes his 'huge focus in training and his competitive spirit,' alongside 'many issues off the field,' including academic struggles and a discovered 'racing heart' condition.

Title Card 'SPORTING 1997 - 2003'.description: Glowing white text on a black background, introducing the time period of Ronaldo’s formative years at Sporting CP, which is directly referenced by the narrator as the period of Cristiano's entry and challenges at the academy.

event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins with a youth football team photo featuring a young Cristiano Ronaldo, highlighting his early days at the Sporting CP academy and his development into a team player, leading to his first professional contract in April 2001. This contract, facilitated by agent José Veiga, was for €2,000 per month with a €20 million release clause. By 2001-2002, at 16, Ronaldo's improved financial standing allowed him to move his mother to Lisbon and train with the first team under manager László Bölöni, despite initial critical reports on his technical, tactical, and physical weaknesses. Bölöni, recognizing Ronaldo's potential despite his 'show-off' tendencies, integrated him into first-team training, though he was still awaiting official match opportunities as Sporting achieved a treble that season. As the 2002-2003 season approached, agent Jorge Mendes strategically planned to represent Ronaldo, Quaresma, and Viana, promising them lucrative moves to major European clubs, and successfully convinced Ronaldo's mother. In September 2002, Ronaldo terminated his contract with Veiga, officially signing with Mendes. With Sporting's main striker Mario Jardel departing and no budget for a replacement, Director of Football Carlos Freitas proposed using Cristiano in the attack. The video then shifts to Ronaldo's progression as a winger, with Bölöni famously predicting he would surpass Eusébio and Figo. Ronaldo scored his first professional goal in a friendly against Real Betis and made his Champions League debut in the 02/03 qualifiers against Inter. His first official league goals followed: one against Moreirense in Superliga GW6 and a winning goal against Boavista in Superliga GW8, showcasing his technical skill and scoring ability. Despite not being a consistent starter at 17, Ronaldo's impactful performances began attracting significant attention.

The narrator mentions him by name, explaining his decision to use Ronaldo as a winger and repeating his prophetic quote to the press.

The narrator explicitly reads out these negatives: 'weak aerial ability, uses weak technique, defensively weak, physically weak, doesn't have tactical awareness as an individual nor as a team player, very selfish, and lacks composure and focus'.

RONALDO' and number 28 visible on his back. The narrator mentions him by name, describes his early academy roles, his first professional goal, Champions League debut, and league goals, while a commentator excitedly shouts "Gol de Ronaldo! Cristiano Ronaldo!" during a celebration.

Title Card 'SUPERLIGA 02/03 - GW8'.description: White text overlay during a goal against Boavista, noting Ronaldo’s 88th-minute goal. The narrator describes this as the winning goal against Boavista.

Title Card 'CHAMPIONS LEAGUE QUALIFYING.description: White text on a dark background, marking Ronaldo’s debut against Inter at 58', with a visual of him entering the pitch. The narrator explicitly mentions his professional debut against Inter Milan in the Champions League qualifiers.

Title Card 'SUPERLIGA 02/03 - GW6'.description: White text overlay during a goal celebration, indicating the match against Moreirense and Ronaldo’s goal times (34', 90+5'). The narrator describes this as his first official league goal against Moreirense.

Archival Football Match Footage.description: Grainy, low-resolution clips of Ronaldo playing in green-and-white striped jerseys, dribbling, scoring, and celebrating during matches, with on-screen text indicating match details like 'SPORTING 3-0 MOREIRENSE' and 'BOAVISTA 1-2 SPORTING'. The footage is accompanied by crowd noise and an excited commentator shouting "Gol de Ronaldo! Cristiano Ronaldo!" during a goal.

raw_segments: The video opens with a black-and-white group photo of a youth football team in green-and-white striped jerseys, featuring a focused Young Cristiano Ronaldo in the back row, marking his early integration into the Sporting CP youth academy. A calm male narrator explains how Cristiano developed from an individual to a team player, leading to his first professional contract. The scene transitions to archival footage of young boys in green Reebok hoodies walking, followed by a close-up of a young Ronaldo in a green training kit, showcasing his early dedication. A split-screen then shows two images of young Ronaldo in Sporting CP jerseys, kneeling with a trophy and holding a stone football trophy, emphasizing his early achievements. Text overlays confirm 'APRIL 2001' as the date of his first professional contract, introduced alongside the Sporting CP crest. The narrator identifies José Veiga as the 'most famous Portuguese agent' at the time, detailing the '4-YEAR DEAL' for '€2,000 PER MONTH', and revealing a hefty €20 million release clause. The video then transitions to a close-up of Ronaldo’s face with '2001-2002' overlaid, as the narrator describes how Cristiano, at 16, became financially capable of moving his mother to Lisbon and got closer to first-team football. Scenes follow of him walking with a man near a stadium and training in a blue Reebok jersey. The segment concludes with a formal portrait of László Bölöni, labeled 'SPORTING MANAGER', with the narrator identifying him as the 'Romanian manager' who called Ronaldo up to train with the first team. Throughout, a steady, narrative background music plays.

The segment opens with a formal portrait of László Bölöni, identified as 'SPORTING MANAGER', standing beside a young Cristiano Ronaldo in a Reebok tracksuit, set against a dramatic, slightly melancholic orchestral score. A handwritten 'Report: Cristiano Ronaldo 2001-2002' then appears on lined paper, as a narrator details its critical findings: "weak aerial ability, uses weak technique, defensively weak, physically weak, doesn't have tactical awareness as an individual nor as a team player, very selfish, and lacks composure and focus." Archival footage shows Ronaldo training with teammates in Reebok gear, jogging and running drills on a grassy field under overcast skies, including a close-up of Bölöni giving instructions to Ronaldo during a rainy session. The narrator explains that "after months and months of training, Cristiano managed to settle in with the first team in the training sessions," but Bölöni "still thought it was not the right time for him to play in official matches," as Ronaldo was "still showing off some tricks that had no place in actual matches, let alone in training sessions," and Bölöni "even threatened not to call up Ronaldo if he didn't stop." The music shifts to a more triumphant tone as the scene transitions to a celebratory moment with Ronaldo and teammates on a 'LISBOA VISION' bus, surrounded by cheering fans waving green-and-white flags, indicating a victory parade. The narrator confirms, "Sporting successfully won the league, the cup, and the Super Cup that season," with Bölöni integrating youngsters like Quaresma and Viana, but "Ronaldo was still waiting for his chance." Key figures Nuno Gomes and Rui Costa are introduced in a split-screen with the Sporting CP crest, followed by a locker room scene where Ronaldo is physically confronted by a teammate, suggesting internal conflict. The segment concludes with a dramatic introduction of Jorge Mendes, shown in a dark suit and sunglasses with his name displayed prominently against a green grid background, as the narrator states, "As the season had closed its curtains, a Portuguese agent called Jorge Mendes..."

The video opens with a dark green, grid-patterned background featuring the Sporting CP crest, as the narrator introduces Jorge Mendes, seen in a dark suit and sunglasses and labeled 'MENDES', as he "entered into the fray." A hierarchical diagram unfolds, showing Mendes's plan to pick up three Sporting Academy players: Quaresma, Viana, and Cristiano Ronaldo, each in youth football attire, all of whom, the narrator explains, were initially represented by José Veiga, also seen in a suit and labeled 'JOSE VEIGA' below Mendes. Mendes promised these players big clubs and earnings of "no less than a million euros." The scene transitions to a vertical column of European football club crests—AC Milan, Juventus, Inter, Real Madrid, Barcelona, Liverpool, Arsenal, and Manchester United—flanked by euro symbols, suggesting transfer negotiations. The focus narrows to Cristiano Ronaldo and Mendes, then shifts to Ronaldo with his mother, as the narrator explains Mendes convinced her of his ambitions. José Veiga is then shown with his mother, as the text 'SEPTEMBER 2002' appears, marking when, the narrator states, "Cristiano terminated his contract with Vega, and thus Mendes was now his agent." The timeline expands to '2002-2003', and the narrator explains that Sporting's main striker, Mario Jardel, seen in a Sporting CP jersey, intended to leave. A man in a beige suit is then seen pointing at a tactical football formation, as the narrator mentions "Boloni" (the manager) needed a new attacker. The name 'FREITAS' appears above a man in a suit, identified by the narrator as "Director of Football Carlos Freitas," who informed the manager there was no budget for a new player and proposed using Cristiano, whose name 'CRISTIANO' appears below Freitas. The segment concludes with archival footage of a young Ronaldo in a yellow-and-black training kit performing a wide leg stretch on a grass field, visually reinforcing his early physical training.

The video opens with archival training footage of a young Cristiano Ronaldo in a yellow-and-black kit performing a wide leg stretch on a grass field, as a narrator explains how a manager believed the teenager improved immensely, noting that in the academy, Cristiano played as a central or second striker. The scene transitions to dynamic match footage where Ronaldo, in a green-and-white striped Sporting CP jersey, dribbles past defenders. A title card displays a quote from László Bölöni, who is shown in a formal portrait beside young Ronaldo, stating, "He’ll be better than Eusébio and Figo," a sentiment echoed by the narrator who explains Bölöni decided to use him as a tricky winger and hyped up the press with this prediction. The narrator recounts how the teenager scored his first professional goal in a friendly against Real Betis. The narrative continues with match highlights from the 2002-2003 season, showing Ronaldo entering the pitch for his Champions League debut against Inter, marked by an on-screen title card 'CHAMPIONS LEAGUE QUALIFYING - 02/03', as the narrator confirms his professional debut in the qualifiers. The narrator then details his first official league goal against Moreirense in October, followed by another goal against Boavista in the dying minutes, which was the winning goal. Visually, on-screen text marks these events as 'SUPERLIGA 02/03 - GW6' (Moreirense, 34', 90+5') and 'SUPERLIGA 02/03 - GW8' (Boavista, 88'), showcasing Ronaldo’s technical skill, including a close-up of a sliding tackle and a powerful shot leading to a goal. A commentator's excited shout of "Gol de Ronaldo! Cristiano Ronaldo!" accompanies a goal celebration, with crowd noise in the background. The segment concludes with Ronaldo walking confidently on the pitch, his jersey displaying 'C. RONALDO' and the number 28, as the narrator reflects on his promising start, acknowledging that while he didn't start consistently, it was normal for a 17-year-old and enough to attract attention.

event_blocks.summary: A young Cristiano Ronaldo, initially seen in a Sporting CP jersey and later in a Reebok training kit, attracted significant European club interest, prompting his agent, Jorge Mendes, to warn Sporting he would leave if not a starter. A narrator detailed this, showcasing logos of Juventus, Inter, Real Madrid, Liverpool, Arsenal, Barcelona, and Valencia, while quoting Ronaldo's pleasure at the interest and his dream to play in Spain or England. Paco Roig, a Valencia presidency candidate, agreed with Mendes to sign Ronaldo and Quaresma for €9.00M if elected. Liverpool Manager Gérard Houllier was shown with an €8.00M offer for Ronaldo, whom the narrator quoted as calling Liverpool 'one of the best clubs in Europe.' Later, Ronaldo, in a Portugal jersey, expressed his dream of playing for a club with tradition, a sentiment echoed by Inter legend Luis Suárez, who urged his club to sign him, believing he'd be one of the world's best. However, Inter's president Moratti was unconvinced by the 16-year-old, and no official offer was made, despite Inter being a high spender. Juventus also pursued Ronaldo, but their striker Marcelo Salas refused a trade deal with Sporting. Parma offered €8.00M for the player, while Real Madrid's Academy head, Ramon Martinez, inquired about him. Barcelona showed interest in Mendes's clients, including Ronaldo, Quaresma, Tiago, and Deco, ultimately prioritizing Quaresma with a preferential contract.

raw_segments: The video opens with archival footage of a young Cristiano Ronaldo in a green-and-white striped Sporting CP jersey, walking on a grass field, followed by a close-up emphasizing his youthful features. He then appears in a beige Reebok training kit, stretching and interacting with teammates during a training session. A male narrator's voice begins, explaining how Ronaldo attracted the attention of several European clubs and how his agent, Jorge Mendes, warned Sporting that Ronaldo would leave if not a starter. The scene transitions to a stylized graphic sequence featuring a smiling man in a suit, identified as the narrator, with logos of major European clubs—Juventus, Inter, Real Madrid, Liverpool, Arsenal, Barcelona, and Valencia—appearing behind him. Text overlays quote Ronaldo, stating he's 'very pleased to hear many important clubs are interested in me' and that his 'dream is to play in Spain or England,' a sentiment directly echoed by the narrator who quotes the 'Portuguese teenager' expressing his pleasure and dreams. The focus then shifts to Paco Roig, a candidate for Valencia presidency, shown in a suit with the Valencia CF crest, alongside a displayed €9.00M transfer fee, as the narrator details Roig's agreement with Mendes to sign both Cristiano and Quaresma for 9 million euros if he won the presidency. Next, Gérard Houllier, labeled as Liverpool Manager, appears with the Liverpool FC crest and a €8.00M fee, with Ronaldo in a red Portugal jersey, as the narrator confirms Liverpool's interest and their 8 million euro offer. The visuals maintain a dark, textured background with grid patterns, emphasizing the high-stakes transfer market, while the narrator concludes by quoting Ronaldo's reaction to Liverpool's offer, calling them 'one of the best clubs in Europe.'

The segment opens with a close-up of a young Cristiano Ronaldo in a red Portugal national team jersey with the number 7, set against a dark red background featuring the Manchester United crest, as a male narrator voices Ronaldo's quote about it being 'a dream to be able to play for a club that represents so much tradition.' The scene transitions to Luis Suárez, identified as an Inter legend and Moratti's consultant, shown in a suit with a serious expression, as the narrator recounts Suárez's strong conviction after seeing Ronaldo in action: 'we have to sign him. He’ll be one of the best players in the world.' Despite this, the narrator explains that Inter's president, Moratti, was not convinced by the 16-year-old Ronaldo, and Inter, despite being one of the biggest spenders, did not make an official offer, while a smiling man with glasses is seen alongside a young player in an Inter Milan jersey. Juventus's efforts to sign the young Portuguese are then highlighted visually with a man in a suit next to a player in a Juventus jersey, with the Juventus logo in the background, as the narrator details how their Chilean striker Marcelo Salas refused to go to Sporting in a trade deal. A split-screen then displays a young Ronaldo in a black-and-white striped Parma jersey with '€8.00M' displayed, as the narrator confirms Italian darlings Parma 'offered 8 million euros for the player.' Simultaneously, the Real Madrid crest appears on the right, with a man in a suit gesturing thoughtfully, as the narrator reveals Real Madrid's Academy head, Ramon Martinez, met with Sporting to inquire about Ronaldo. The final frames focus on Barcelona's interest, showing a man in a suit with glasses pointing against a purple background with the FC Barcelona crest, while the narrator explains Barcelona was interested in Jorge Mendes's clients, including Quaresma, Tiago, Deco, and Ronaldo, but ultimately 'pushed to sign Quaresma and offered a preferential contract,' as framed photos of these players, with a focus on Quaresma and Cristiano, are sequentially displayed.

event_blocks.summary: Despite advanced negotiations for Cristiano Ronaldo, the deal ultimately failed, as Jorge Mendes confirmed that Arsenal had the most advanced talks, stating, 'THERE WAS A TIME WHEN I REALLY THOUGHT HE WAS GOING TO ARSENAL.' In January 2003, Ronaldo and his mother flew to London to visit Arsenal's training ground, meeting Arsène Wenger and Thierry Henry, with Ronaldo reportedly falling in love with the club due to its philosophy and the care shown to young players, as detailed in Guillem Balagué's biography. Arsenal had agreed on all terms with Sporting CP, with Wenger reserving the number 9 shirt for Ronaldo, as indicated by a jersey displayed between photos of Wenger and Ronaldo. However, Arsenal's vice president David Dein's trip to Lisbon to finalize the deal was unsuccessful because the offered price was too low for Sporting, a situation exacerbated by Arsenal's financial constraints stemming from the construction of the Emirates Stadium.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a split-screen graphic featuring framed photos of young Cristiano Ronaldo and Quaresma, set against an FC Barcelona crest, while a dramatic, investigative background music plays. A narrator begins by stating that despite advanced talks for Cristiano, it 'amounted to nothing.' The scene transitions to a dark red backdrop with the Arsenal crest, where Jorge Mendes, a man in a dark suit, appears smiling, accompanied by an overlay quote attributed to him: 'THERE WAS A TIME WHEN I REALLY THOUGHT HE WAS GOING TO ARSENAL.' The narrator confirms Mendes's statement, highlighting Arsenal as the club with the most advanced talks. The timeline shifts to 'JAN 2003,' showing Ronaldo’s photo alongside his mother’s photo and the Arsenal crest, with Arsène Wenger’s photo then added, as the narrator details Cristiano and his mother flying to London to visit Arsenal's training facility and meeting Wenger and Thierry Henry. A full-screen image of Ronaldo in a red Portugal jersey appears next to the book cover 'Cristiano Ronaldo: The Biography' by Guillem Balagué, with a quote: 'CRISTIANO FELL IN LOVE WITH THE CLUB.' Balagué, a man with a beard, is then shown smiling next to another quote from him: 'THE PHILOSOPHY AND THE CARE WITH WHICH YOUNG PLAYERS ARE TREATED,' with the narrator attributing these insights to Ronaldo's biography. A diagram then connects Wenger, David Dein, and João de Freitas to the Arsenal crest, followed by a red Arsenal jersey displaying 'RONALDO 9' between Wenger and Ronaldo’s photo, as the narrator reveals Arsenal had agreed on all terms with Sporting and Wenger even kept the number 9 shirt for Cristiano. The scene shifts to a green background with the Sporting CP crest, showing Dein and Freitas in framed photos with a jagged white line symbolizing a broken connection, as the narrator explains that Arsenal's vice president David Dein went to Lisbon to seal the deal but offered too low a price for Sporting. The segment concludes with aerial footage of London, focusing on the Emirates Stadium and surrounding urban landscape, while the narrator mentions Arsenal's financial restrictions due to the construction of the new Emirates Stadium.

raw_segments: The segment opens with an aerial view of Emirates Stadium, followed by a bold title card, 'THE UNITED STORY SUMMER 2003', in glowing red text on a black background. A subtle, dramatic orchestral score plays as a male narrator begins, discussing an English club's successful negotiations. The scene transitions to archival football footage of a young Cristiano Ronaldo, wearing a green-and-white striped Sporting CP jersey with 'PT' sponsorship, competing against a Manchester United player in a red 'vodafone' jersey. A referee's whistle sound briefly cuts through the music. The narrator explains that while many believe Ronaldo's transfer was due to his performance against the 'Red Devils' in a friendly in August 2003, there was significant prior planning from United. A red-toned graphic displaying '2002-2003' appears as the narrator details how, in that season, Sir Alex Ferguson's assistant, the Portuguese Carlos Queiroz, received reports on Cristiano and knew the club needed to act quickly. Carlos Queiroz is then shown in a red training top, identified as 'ASSISTANT MANAGER - UNITED', and his calm, slightly accented voice is heard stating, "I didn't take on any responsibilities in terms of recruiting young players, but I informed Alex about Cristiano." The video then juxtaposes Queiroz with Sir Alex Ferguson, highlighting their relationship. A flashback shows Queiroz in a green jacket with the Sporting CP crest and the years '94-96', followed by him on the bench writing notes, as the narrator explains Queiroz was Sporting Lisbon's manager during that period and used his connections to arrange a special agreement for exchanging methodologies and ideas. The segment concludes with a split-screen graphic showing the Manchester United and Sporting CP crests with bidirectional arrows, symbolizing this transfer relationship.

The segment opens with a split-screen graphic displaying the Manchester United and Sporting CP crests with bidirectional arrows, set against a backdrop of tense, dramatic instrumental music, as a narrator explains how certain deals would become much easier. The scene transitions to Jim Ryan, Reserve Team Coach at United, standing beside Sir Alex Ferguson, who gestures thoughtfully. Ryan, in a clear, slightly older male voice, is heard stating, "I've seen a player... I think he's a winger, but he's playing as a central striker with the youth team," followed by "I wouldn't wait long to declare our interest. There will be other clubs after him," a quote also displayed as text overlay. The narration continues, detailing how Ferguson managed to send Ryan to Sporting Lisbon's facilities and later suggested signing Cristiano Ronaldo to Sporting's management, with a condition of a loan-back, a common request from other competing clubs like Juventus, Real Madrid, Liverpool, Arsenal, Barcelona, and Valencia, whose crests appear in a montage. Ferguson is then seen on a phone call, with a photo of young Cristiano Ronaldo beside him, an arrow pointing to the Sporting CP crest, signifying United's pursuit. The narrator explains that a special agreement between Sporting Lisbon and Manchester United led to booking a friendly match to celebrate the opening of Sporting's new stadium, Estádio José Alvalade, a 3D model of which is shown with the date August 6, 2003. The video cuts to a real-life moment of Ferguson and Ronaldo walking near a bridge, then back to a graphic of the two clubs facing off at the stadium. The final frames show a split-screen of Ferguson and Jorge Mendes, identified by the narrator as the man in sunglasses, on August 5, 2003, underscoring the critical timing of their meeting a day before the friendly, as United's interest in Cristiano intensified.

event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins with a stylized graphic of Sir Alex Ferguson, introducing Manchester United's past transfer misses before transitioning to a split-screen comparing Sporting CP and Manchester United elements, featuring portraits of agents Jorge Mendes and José Veiga alongside Ferguson and a young Cristiano Ronaldo, with a Sporting CP representative symbolizing negotiations. The narration reveals Sporting's awareness of Ronaldo and Mendes' disinterest in contract renewal, despite assurances from new manager Fernando Santos. Ferguson, seen on a training ground and interviewed, is presented as seeking 'new blood,' with Ronaldo fitting that role, while Mendes recognized the deal's potential to elevate his career. The transfer was mutually beneficial, with only the fee remaining, as Ferguson believed United could outbid rivals. Archival footage shows Ronaldo scoring for Sporting CP, with commentator praise for his skills and the anticipation of his superstardom, confirmed by Ferguson's quote about the 'biggest surge of excitement.' Carlos Freitas, Sporting's director of football, confirmed United's willingness to pay more for an immediate transfer, leading to a graphic detailing the £12.24 million transfer fee and a five-year contract with a rising salary. Ferguson's promise to Ronaldo about playing time, 'NEXT YEAR YOU'LL PLAY IN HALF THE GAMES,' is highlighted as Ronaldo, initially requesting number 28, was given the iconic number 7 jersey at Manchester United, symbolizing a 'huge transformation.' The narrative emphasizes the legacy of the number 7 shirt, worn by legends like George Best and David Beckham. Despite United being four-time league champions, Ferguson sought 'youthful energy,' leading to Ronaldo's debut against Bolton, where he won a penalty and was voted player of the match, culminating in his national team debut for Portugal under Luis Scolari.

Jorge Mendes.description: Appears in a framed portrait on the left side of the split-screen, wearing a dark suit, representing Sporting CP’s agent side in the negotiation graphic. The narrator confirms his involvement, stating 'Ronaldo and Mendes were not interested in renewing their deal' and that 'Mendes knew that sealing this deal would catapult him to the forefront of all football deals.'

Sporting Portugal Crest.description: Shown on the green side of the split-screen, with a lion emblem and green-and-white stripes, representing Ronaldo’s home club. The narrator states 'Sporting knew that Ronaldo and Mendes were not interested in renewing their deal.'

Cristiano Ronaldo.description: Appears in multiple forms: first in a red Manchester United jersey (number 7) walking away from the camera, then smiling with a teammate in a red training kit, later in a red jersey celebrating on the pitch, and finally in a Portugal national team jersey (number 11) during a match, with a focused expression. The narrator frequently refers to him by name, detailing his 'huge transformation,' his introduction to the media, his debut against Bolton where he won a penalty and was voted player of the match, and his subsequent national team debut with Portugal.

Manchester United Crest.description: Displayed prominently above Ferguson’s portrait on the red side of the split-screen, symbolizing the club’s interest in Ronaldo. The narrator frequently mentions 'United's offer,' 'Ferguson and United,' and Ronaldo's intent 'to show Manchester United what they were getting.'

Archival Football Match Footage.description: Grainy, low-resolution clips of Ronaldo playing in a beige and black Sporting CP jersey, dribbling past opponents in red jerseys on a green pitch, with visible sponsor banners in the background, accompanied by live commentary praising his 'unbelievably quick feet' and predicting he's 'gonna be a superstar' and 'exactly what he can do'.

Sir Alex Ferguson.description: Shown lifting the Premier League trophy amidst confetti, then in a formal photo with Ronaldo and a teammate, wearing a suit and red tie, smiling. The narrator mentions Ferguson's strategic thinking, noting he 'thought that the team needed a bit of youthful energy' and 'made changes while his teams were at the top.'

Quote Text 'NEXT YEAR YOU'LL PLAY IN HAL.description: White text overlaid on a red grid background, attributed to Sir Alex Ferguson, sourced from 'Balague, Guillem. Cristiano Ronaldo: The Biography'.

Manchester United Jersey.description: A red short-sleeved jersey with 'vodafone' sponsorship, worn by Ronaldo during an interview and as he walks away, with the number 7 visible on the back. The narrator emphasizes the 'honor of wearing the number 7 shirt at United,' highlighting its legendary status.

Transfer Fee Graphic.description: A red grid background with '£12.24M' displayed prominently, accompanied by a timeline from 03/04 to 07/08 and a salary of '€24,000' below, with Ronaldo in a Portugal jersey (number 7) on the left, which the narrator details as a final price of '12.2 million pounds' for a five-year contract, with his yearly salary increasing from '24,000 euros' to '2 million euros'.

Quote Text 'THE BIGGEST SURGE OF EXCITEM.description: White text overlaid on a red grid background, attributed to Sir Alex Ferguson, sourced from 'Balague, Guillem. Cristiano Ronaldo: The Biography'.

Carlos Freitas.description: Appears in a close-up with his quote 'THEIR PRIORITIES CHANGED AND THEY SAID 'WE'LL PAY MORE, BUT HE'S COMING STRAIGHT AWAY'' overlaid, and is identified by the narrator as the 'director of football Freitas' who confirmed United's decision to pay more than the agreed fee for an immediate transfer.

Portugal National Team Jersey.description: A maroon long-sleeved jersey with yellow trim and the number 11, worn by Ronaldo during a match, featuring the Portuguese crest and Nike logo. The narrator mentions Ronaldo's 'national team debut with Portugal' while wearing this jersey.

Title Card 'UNITED 2003 -'.description: Glowing red text on a black background, introducing the timeline of Manchester United’s 2003 season.

Champions Banner.description: A large red-and-white banner with 'CHAMPIONS' in bold white letters, held by fans in a stadium, celebrating a title win.

Graphic of Past United Legends.description: A stylized red grid background with the number 7, overlaid with black-and-white portraits of Coppell, Best, Robson, and Becks, each labeled with their names in white text, symbolizing the legacy of the number 7 jersey. The narrator explicitly names 'George Best, Steve Coppell, Brian Robson, Eric Cantona, and David Beckham' as 'United legends' who wore this 'legendary number.'

raw_segments: The segment opens with a dramatic, stylized graphic featuring Sir Alex Ferguson's face in a white-bordered frame against a textured red background with a grid overlay, setting a serious, strategic tone. A narrator's voice details Manchester United's past transfer misses, including Beckham, Veron, and Ronaldinho, before stating, 'So now let's see what's on the table.' The scene evolves into a split-screen format, with the left side displaying the Sporting Portugal crest and a green background, while the right side remains red. Framed portraits appear on the left: first, Jorge Mendes in a dark suit, followed by José Veiga in a light shirt. On the right, Ferguson’s portrait reappears, now under the Manchester United crest, followed by a young Cristiano Ronaldo in a green-and-white Sporting CP jersey. A third figure, a Man in Sunglasses (Sporting CP Representative) in a dark suit holding a phone, appears on the far right, symbolizing the negotiation side. The narrator explains that 'Sporting knew that Ronaldo and Mendes were not interested in renewing their deal,' despite new manager Fernando Santos pledging to play Cristiano as a starter. The graphic transitions to real-world footage: Ferguson smiling on a sunny training ground, then being interviewed by reporters on the pitch, wearing a grey Manchester United training shirt with 'vodafone' sponsorship. The narrator adds that 'Ferguson and United wanted to inject some new blood into the team, and Cristiano filled that bill.' The narration continues, stating that 'Mendes knew that sealing this deal would catapult him to the forefront of all football deals,' and that 'the deal made sense for each side,' with only 'the figure' missing, as Ferguson 'knew that United can outbid any other club.' The scene cuts to grainy archival footage of Ronaldo in action—celebrating a goal in a black-and-white Sporting CP kit, then playing in a green-and-white striped jersey, dribbling past opponents and scoring, with the ball hitting the net, and visible sponsor banners like 'Coca-Cola' and 'Varilux' in the background. Over this, the narrator reveals that 'Cristiano knew that the deal was agreed before the booked friendly,' and he 'entered the match to show Manchester United what they were getting.' Live commentators then enthusiastically describe Ronaldo's play, praising a 'lovely little trick that he has' and noting how he 'swings one,' with an opponent 'couldn't get near him,' all underscored by energetic instrumental music.

The video opens with archival football match footage showing a young Cristiano Ronaldo in a beige and black Sporting CP jersey (number 28) skillfully dribbling past opponents in red jerseys on a green pitch, as a commentator praises his 'unbelievably quick feet' and declares he's 'gonna be a superstar.' The scene transitions to a close-up of Ronaldo in the same jersey, followed by a quote from Sir Alex Ferguson overlaid on a red grid background: 'THE BIGGEST SURGE OF EXCITEMENT AND ANTICIPATION I EXPERIENCED IN FOOTBALL MANAGEMENT,' which a narrator confirms was Ferguson's reaction to seeing Ronaldo play live. The next frame shows Ferguson in a suit, seated on the bench with a focused expression, flanked by players. This is followed by a quote from Carlos Freitas: 'THEIR PRIORITIES CHANGED AND THEY SAID 'WE'LL PAY MORE, BUT HE'S COMING STRAIGHT AWAY',' displayed over a dark green grid background, as the narrator identifies Freitas as the director of football who confirmed United's decision to pay more for an immediate transfer. A graphic then appears with Ronaldo in a Portugal jersey (number 7) and a transfer fee of '£12.24M' alongside a timeline from 03/04 to 07/08, with a salary of '€24,000' shown below, which the narrator elaborates was a final price of 12.2 million pounds for a five-year contract, with Ronaldo's yearly salary increasing to 2 million euros. Another Ferguson quote follows: 'NEXT YEAR YOU'LL PLAY IN HALF THE GAMES,' as he is shown smiling while on a phone call, with the narrator confirming this was Ferguson's promise regarding Ronaldo's playing time. The final frames show Ronaldo in a red Manchester United jersey (number 7) being interviewed, with a microphone held close to him, and then walking away from the camera, his back to the viewer, as he moves through a stadium with red seats, symbolizing his arrival at the club after initially asking for number 28 but being given number 7.

The video opens with a close-up of Cristiano Ronaldo’s back, wearing a red Manchester United jersey with 'RONALDO' and the number 7, as he walks away from the camera. A narrator speaks about the "honor of wearing the number 7 shirt at United," a "legendary number." This transitions into a stylized graphic with the number 7, overlaid with black-and-white portraits of past United legends—Coppell, Best, Robson, and Becks—as the narrator explicitly names "George Best, Steve Coppell, Brian Robson, Eric Cantona, and David Beckham" as those who wore it, emphasizing the jersey's legacy. The scene shifts to a smiling Ronaldo, arm-in-arm with a teammate in a red training kit, set against the backdrop of a stadium with red seats, as the narrator discusses "a huge transformation in Cristiano's life." A black screen follows, then a glowing title card reads 'UNITED 2003 -', while the background music becomes more rhythmic and driving. The narrator explains that "United were four-time league champions" but "Ferguson thought that the team needed a bit of youthful energy." This leads into a celebratory moment where fans wave a 'CHAMPIONS' banner, followed by Sir Alex Ferguson lifting the Premier League trophy amidst confetti, and Ronaldo in a red jersey celebrating with teammates. A formal photo shows Ronaldo, Ferguson, and a teammate posing together in front of a Manchester United backdrop. The narrator details that "four days after Cristiano was introduced to the media, he was given his debut" against Bolton, with "excitement in the stands" palpable, accompanied by a crowd cheering sound. The video then cuts to Ronaldo in a red jersey walking on the pitch, followed by a close-up of his face as he runs, as the narrator states "Cristiano managed to win a penalty for the team and was voted the player of the match." The final frames show Ronaldo in a Portugal national team jersey (number 11) during a match, with a focused expression under stadium lights, as the narrator concludes that "Luis Scolari gave Cristiano his national team debut with Portugal as well."

Cristiano Ronaldo.description: Appears in multiple forms: first in a red Manchester United jersey (number 7) during match action against Arsenal, where his "skill to escape Arsenal's pressure" is noted by the narrator, then in a white training top with 'RONALDO' on the back during a hug with Ferguson, and later in a red jersey celebrating a goal against Portsmouth, which a commentator excitedly calls "Cristiano Ronaldo's first goal for United!" His appearance evolves from a focused youth to a dynamic professional player, with the narrator highlighting his "right mindset for any professional" despite initial "lack of tactical awareness and teamwork."

Title Card 'WOLVES - UNITED JAN 17, 2004.description: White text on a black background, displayed on a retro-style screen, introducing a match highlight where Ronaldo scores a goal, which the narrator describes as a "speculative shot from distance" and a "bad example" of his early play.

Title Card 'UNITED - CHELSEA SEP 21, 200.description: White text on a black background, displayed on the retro-style screen, introducing match footage of Ronaldo in action against Chelsea, where he demonstrates "great bit of skill to escape Chelsea's press," presented as a "good example."

Title Card 'UNITED - FULHAM OCT 25, 2003.description: White text on a black background, displayed on the retro-style screen, introducing a free-kick goal by Ronaldo, which the narrator praises as "a great free kick with a different technique than the one he uses today" and a "good example."

Title Card 'UNITED - ARSENAL SEP 21, 200.description: White text on a black background, displayed on a retro-style screen with a red border, introducing the match footage of Ronaldo in action against Arsenal.

Archival Football Match Footage.description: Grainy, low-resolution clips of Ronaldo playing in a red Manchester United jersey, dribbling, shooting, and being tackled, with visible stadium crowds and match details on-screen. The footage captures his early professional performances, accompanied by match sounds and commentary snippets, including an excited call for "Cristiano Ronaldo's first goal for United!" and a commentator's shout of "It's been turned in by Costinha!" during the Champions League exit context.

Manchester United Jersey.description: A red short-sleeved jersey with 'vodafone' sponsorship, worn by Ronaldo during match action, with the number 7 visible on the back. The jersey is shown in various match contexts, including during dribbling, shooting, and celebrating.

Roy Keane.description: Appears in a black training top with the Manchester United crest, shown hugging Ronaldo and later in a close-up with a serious expression, delivering a quote about Ronaldo’s work ethic. The narrator directly quotes him saying Ronaldo "was immediately one of the hardest working players at United."

Title Card '03/04 PREMIER LEAGUE - GW11.description: White text on a black background, displayed on a retro-style screen, introducing the goal celebration footage.

Quote Text 'HE WAS IMMEDIATELY ONE OF TH.description: White text overlaid on a red grid background, attributed to Roy Keane, sourced from 'Balague, Guillem. Cristiano Ronaldo: The Biography'. The narrator explicitly states, "As Roy Keane had said, he was immediately one of the hardest working players at United."

EPL 03/04 Standings Graphic.description: A red grid background with white text listing the top four teams: Arsenal (90), Chelsea (79), United (75), and Liverpool (60), with United highlighted in a white bar.

Sir Alex Ferguson.description: Shown in a grey training top hugging Ronaldo, then in a dark blue jacket clapping. The narrator describes him as treating Ronaldo "just like he treated Cantona in the past, different from the other players," and knowing "that despite his lack of tactical awareness and teamwork, Cristiano had the right mindset for any professional."

raw_segments: The video opens with a black-and-white shot of a young Cristiano Ronaldo, number 7, alongside another player in Manchester United training gear, as a male narrator with a serious tone describes Cristiano's promising start but notes his teammates were not convinced of his playstyle, leading to him receiving tough, physical treatment and reacting negatively by diving and flopping. The visuals transition to a focused close-up of Ronaldo in a white training top, then dynamic match footage of him in a red Manchester United jersey, dribbling, shooting, and being tackled, illustrating the narrator's point that he earned a reputation for wanting to show off and needing to drastically change his playstyle. The scene shifts to a stylized, grainy retro-style screen displaying match highlights from his first season with United, which the narrator introduces as "bad and good examples," all set against a backdrop of dramatic instrumental music. We see a 'WOLVES - UNITED, JAN 17, 2004' title card followed by Ronaldo scoring a powerful goal, which the narrator critiques as a "speculative shot from distance" while United were losing. Another clip shows Ronaldo being tackled, described as demonstrating his "great pace but weak physical strength" as a defender pushes him out. The narrative then highlights positive examples, with a 'UNITED - FULHAM, OCT 25, 2003' title card preceding Ronaldo scoring a free-kick goal, praised as "a great free kick with a different technique than the one he uses today." Further clips include 'UNITED - CHELSEA, SEP 21, 2003', where Ronaldo displays "great bit of skill to escape Chelsea's press," and 'UNITED - ARSENAL, SEP 21, 2003', showing him dribbling past a defender, which the narrator begins to describe as a "great bit of" skill before the audio cuts off.

The video opens with archival match footage from September 21, 2003, showing a young Cristiano Ronaldo in his red Manchester United number 7 jersey skillfully dribbling past an Arsenal defender, though the narrator notes his "skill to escape Arsenal's pressure" was "followed by a poor pass forward" amidst the sounds of a football match. This visual narrative of mentorship is underscored by a split-screen of Roy Keane and Sir Alex Ferguson embracing Ronaldo, with the narrator explaining that "United had a completely different philosophy, but Ferguson treated the teenager just like he treated Cantona in the past, different from the other players." A close-up of Ferguson embracing Ronaldo in a white training top further emphasizes this connection. A quote from Roy Keane, displayed over a red grid background with his image, states: 'He was immediately one of the hardest working players at United,' a sentiment echoed by the narrator who adds that "Fergie knew that despite his lack of tactical awareness and teamwork, Cristiano had the right mindset for any professional." The narrative shifts to Ronaldo scoring his first goal against Portsmouth, with on-screen text 'UNITED 3-0 P'MOUTH, RONALDO - 80’', as a commentator excitedly announces, "Cristiano Ronaldo's first goal for United!" and the narrator confirms it came "through a free kick against Portsmouth." A graphic then highlights Ronaldo’s early stats: 'UNTIL JANUARY, 19 CAPS, 11 STARTS', with the narrator confirming he "managed to participate in 19 matches, starting in 11," followed by a shot of Sir Alex Ferguson clapping. The segment concludes with the narrator explaining that "United were in a transitional period that season," having "crashed out of the round of 16 of the Champions League against Porto and Mourinho," with a commentator's excited shout of "It's been turned in by Costinha!" likely referencing that Champions League exit. Despite this, a graphic shows the EPL 03/04 standings with United in third place, and Ronaldo's growing impact is reinforced by a quick shot of him scoring a goal against Chelsea, as the narrator states "In the league, United ended the season in third position."

event_blocks.summary: The EPL 03/04 standings graphic shows Manchester United in third place with 75 points, behind Arsenal and Chelsea, as a narrator explains United finished behind an improved Chelsea and an unbeaten Arsenal. Sir Alex Ferguson embraces a young Cristiano Ronaldo in his Manchester United #7 jersey. A tactical formation graphic displays United's 4-4-2 setup with Ronaldo on the right wing, alongside Van Nistelrooy, Scholes, and Keane, as the narrator notes Ferguson started Ronaldo to give him a taste of trophy competition in the FA Cup final against Millwall. Live match footage shows Ronaldo, looking much improved and outstanding, with a commentator exclaiming, "Oh, a wonderful start from the Portuguese young international!" amidst crowd noise. Ronaldo scores in the 44th minute, confirmed by the title card '2004 FA CUP FINAL UNITED 1-0 MILLWALL RONALDO - 44', with the commentator shouting "Ronaldo!" and the crowd roaring. United wins the match 3-0, as confirmed by the title card 'UNITED 3-0 MILLWALL 16 FULL TIME', and the commentator states, "It's United's year for the FA Cup again." A black screen with glowing text 'EURO SUMMER 2004' and melancholic music transitions to a portrait of Luiz Scolari, who gave Cristiano national team caps before the Euros held in Portugal. The Portugal squad list highlights Ronaldo as number 17, followed by footage of him training with the national team, laughing with teammates. The narrator states Cristiano successfully joined Scolari's final squad, becoming the sixth youngest player in the competition's history, arriving at the training camp full of confidence. The video concludes with Scolari's quote: 'He’s a good kid, but someone must’ve told him he’s the best in the world.'

Title Card '2004 FA CUP FINAL UNITED 1-0.description: White text on a black background, displayed during the goal celebration, indicating the match, score, and Ronaldo’s goal time.

Luiz Scolari.description: A portrait of the Portuguese manager with a mustache, wearing a dark blue jacket, shown with a serious expression, later accompanied by a quote criticizing Ronaldo’s perceived overconfidence. His name is spoken by the narrator.

Title Card 'EURO SUMMER 2004'.description: Glowing white text on a black background, serving as a transition to the Euro 2004 segment.

Quote Text 'HE’S A GOOD KID, BUT SOMEONE.description: White text overlaid on a red grid background, attributed to Luiz Scolari, sourced from 'Balague, Guillem. Cristiano Ronaldo: The Biography', highlighting early skepticism about Ronaldo’s confidence.

Title Card 'UNITED 3-0 MILLWALL 16 FULL.description: White text on a black background, displayed during the trophy lift, indicating the final score and time.

Cristiano Ronaldo.description: Appears in a Manchester United red jersey with 'RONALDO 7' on the back during match action and celebration, then in a white training top with 'SAGRES' branding during national team training, showcasing his evolution from a young professional to an emerging international star. His name is frequently mentioned by the narrator and shouted by a commentator during his goal.

EPL 03/04 Standings Graphic.description: A red grid background with white text listing the top four teams: Arsenal (90), Chelsea (79), United (75), and Liverpool (60), with United highlighted in a white bar, emphasizing their position in the league.

Portugal Squad List.description: A graphic listing the 23-player squad for Euro 2004, with Ronaldo highlighted as number 17, set against a red grid background with the UEFA Euro 2004 Portugal logo.

Tactical Formation Graphic.description: A white outline of a football pitch with red dots marking player positions, including Van Nistelrooy, Scholes, C. Ronaldo, Keane, Fletcher, O'Shea, Silvestre, Brown, Neville, and Howard, illustrating United's 4-4-2 formation.

Archival Football Match Footage.description: Grainy, low-resolution clips of Ronaldo playing in a red Manchester United jersey, dribbling, shooting, and being tackled, with visible stadium crowds and match details on-screen, capturing his early professional performances.

Sir Alex Ferguson.description: Wearing a dark blue jacket, smiling warmly as he embraces Cristiano Ronaldo, later seen in a grey training top and then in a formal suit with a red tie, symbolizing his role as manager and mentor.

raw_segments: The video opens with an EPL 03/04 standings graphic, highlighting Manchester United in third place with 75 points, behind Arsenal and Chelsea, as a male narrator explains United finished behind "an improved Chelsea side and Arsenal, who remained unbeaten throughout the season." The scene transitions to a warm embrace between Sir Alex Ferguson and a young Cristiano Ronaldo in his Manchester United #7 jersey, symbolizing their mentor-mentee relationship. A tactical formation graphic then shows United's 4-4-2 setup with Ronaldo on the right wing, alongside players like Van Nistelrooy, Scholes, and Keane, as the narrator explains "United got all the way to the FA Cup final as they faced Millwall from the Championship. Ferguson decided to use Cristiano from the start to give him a taste of competing for a trophy." The narrative shifts to live match footage, showing Ronaldo in his red United jersey, with the narrator remarking on his improved performance: "The struggles of the season were behind him, as he looked much better. It could be one of his most important matches in his career, as he was just outstanding." A male commentator then exclaims, "Oh, a wonderful start from the Portuguese young international!" amidst crowd noise, before the narrator states Ronaldo "scored the goal" as live match footage shows him scoring against Millwall in the 44th minute, with the commentator shouting "Ronaldo!" and the crowd roaring in excitement. A title card confirms '2004 FA CUP FINAL UNITED 1-0 MILLWALL RONALDO - 44'. This is followed by a celebration with Ferguson and the team lifting the FA Cup trophy, with the narrator confirming, "As United won the match handsomely by three goals to nil." The commentator adds, "It's United's year for the FA Cup again," as a title card displays 'UNITED 3-0 MILLWALL 16 FULL TIME'. The segment then transitions to a black screen with glowing text 'EURO SUMMER 2004', accompanied by ambient, slightly melancholic music. A portrait of Luiz Scolari appears as the narrator explains, "Luis Scolari gave Cristiano some national team caps before the Euros, which were held in Portugal that summer." The Portugal squad list is shown, with Ronaldo highlighted as number 17, followed by footage of him training with the national team, laughing and interacting with teammates. The narrator continues, "And Cristiano successfully joined Scolari's final squad for the summer tournament and became the sixth youngest player in the competition's history. He arrived at the training camp full of confidence." The video concludes with a quote from Scolari: 'He’s a good kid, but someone must’ve told him he’s the best in the world,' underscoring the early perception of Ronaldo’s confidence and talent.

event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins with a quote from Luiz Scolari, displayed as bold white text against a dark red grid, stating, 'As Scolari said, that he's a good kid, but someone must have told him he's the best in the world. And if he thinks he is, it will be very hard to work with him,' accompanied by his portrait and dramatic background music. This transitions to a montage of Cristiano Ronaldo in his Portugal national team jersey, number 17, with Nike branding and a yellow armband, as the narrator explains his increasing minutes in the tournament. Sequential national flags of Greece, Russia, and Spain appear, detailing match results: Portugal's 1-2 loss to Greece where Ronaldo entered in the second half, conceded a penalty, and scored; Russia's 0-2 loss to Portugal with a Ronaldo assist; and Spain's 0-1 loss to Portugal with Ronaldo starting and earning man of the match. Clips show Ronaldo celebrating, frustrated, and intense, followed by Portugal's 2-1 penalty shootout win against England in the quarter-finals, and a win against the Netherlands in the semi-finals where Ronaldo assisted and scored, celebrated shirtless. A black-and-white Portugal squad photo shows Ronaldo in the front row as the narrator notes he became the youngest player to start a final, which ended in a 0-1 loss to Greece, mirroring the opener, with Ronaldo dejected and screaming after missing a crucial chance. The segment then shifts to Ronaldo's return to Manchester United, described as a 'different person,' with Mike Phelan's quote, "His body shape was different, his facial features were different... That’s an athletic physique for the British game," displayed and spoken, highlighting his physical transformation. Footage of Ronaldo in a red Manchester United jersey with 'vodafone' sponsorship running during training emphasizes his athleticism. Gary Neville's quote, "Do you live with him for another 12 months? Where he’s doing OK but not scoring 25 goals? Where he can turn off and not defend?" is presented as Ronaldo's inconsistent performances weighed on him. Grainy archival match footage from 2004-2005 against Fulham, Blackburn, and Bolton shows Ronaldo dribbling, shooting, and scoring, with on-screen graphics detailing match information. The narrator notes Ronaldo's focus on "new skills" despite "wrong decisions" but also "slight improvements" in passing, concluding with his "rocket of a shot" as an excited commentator exclaims, "Now he's got!" amidst crowd cheers. Archival highlights from the 2004-2005 season against Bolton, Fulham, Blackburn, and Bolton showcase Ronaldo's dynamic play, with a narrator exclaiming "the goal! And what a goal!" and noting his skill on his weak foot, as well as a near-miss hitting the crossbar. Close-ups of Ronaldo in his red Manchester United jersey display intense focus, with the narrator explaining his improved physical endurance and ability to overcome defenders. A still image of Sir Alex Ferguson, Carlos Queiroz, and Wayne Rooney, with Rooney holding a 'ROONEY 8' jersey, symbolizes key signings and the return of Queiroz. The competitive landscape is described with José Mourinho's portrait against the Chelsea crest, noting United was "far away from competing" due to Chelsea's spending. EPL 04/05 standings show United in third place, 18 points behind champions Chelsea. A side-by-side image of Ferguson and Arsène Wenger precedes highlights from the 2005 Champions League Round of 16 against AC Milan, with an on-screen graphic 'MILAN 1(2-0)0 UNITED MAR 8, 2005,' and the narrator recounting an "early exit in Europe." FA Cup semifinal highlights against Newcastle show United winning 4-1, indicated by '2005 FA CUP SEMIFINALS N'CASTLE 1-4 UNITED,' concluding with Ronaldo celebrating a goal. The segment opens with Ronaldo smiling with teammates in a black Manchester United training top, as the narrator highlights his 'goal-scoring attributes.' A dynamic match clip features an excited commentator shouting 'Ronaldo! Chance Manchester United! Goal! Manchester United! Cristiano Ronaldo!' amidst crowd cheers and a whistle, with a scoreboard graphic '04/05 PREMIER LEAGUE - GW25 ARSENAL 2-4 UNITED RONALDO - 54' 58''. Ronaldo celebrates with teammates, including Giggs, before the focus shifts to the 'FA Cup Final,' where Ronaldo is seen with a focused expression, described as 'one step faster than Arsenal's solid right-back Lauren' while playing on the left and being the 'number one victim of some rash tackles.' Footage shows Ronaldo dribbling past Lauren and scoring, followed by his celebration, with match commentary noting 'frustration, despair' from Arsenal. Sir Alex Ferguson and Roy Keane are shown on the sidelines as the narrator explains the final was 'settled through penalties' and Ronaldo 'successfully converted his penalty,' though United 'failed to retain the cup' and ended the season 'without a trophy.' The segment concludes with a graphic summarizing Ronaldo’s 2004-2005 season stats (33 PL apps, 5 goals, 4 assists; 8 CL apps, 0 goals, 2 assists; 7 FA Cup apps, 4 goals, 3 assists; 2 EFL Cup apps, 0 goals, 0 assists) with 'AGE: 20' displayed, as the narrator discusses 'Cristiano's numbers for the season' and his 'great performances within the FA Cup.'

Quote Text 'AND IF HE THINKS HE IS, IT’L.description: White text overlaid on a dark red grid background, attributed to Luiz Scolari, emphasizing the manager’s concern about Ronaldo’s ego, and narrated by a clear, slightly accented male voice.

Portugal National Team Jersey.description: A maroon long-sleeved jersey with yellow trim and the number 17, worn by Ronaldo during match action and celebrations, featuring the Portuguese crest and Nike logo.

Match Score Graphics.description: On-screen text displaying match results such as 'MILAN 1(2-0)0 UNITED' and 'N'CASTLE 1-4 UNITED', positioned with match dates and competition names, indicating key fixtures, which are also referenced by the narrator.

Luiz Scolari.description: A portrait of the Portuguese manager with a mustache, wearing a dark blue jacket, shown with a serious expression, accompanied by a critical quote about Ronaldo’s perceived overconfidence, which is narrated by a clear, slightly accented male voice.

National Flags.description: Circular icons of the flags of Greece, Russia, Spain, England, and Ukraine, appearing sequentially to represent opponents in Euro 2004 matches, displayed against the red grid background, with the male narrator identifying the corresponding countries as opponents (also mentioning the Netherlands in the semi-finals).

Portugal Squad List.description: A graphic listing the 23-player squad for Euro 2004, with Ronaldo highlighted as number 17, set against a red grid background with the UEFA Euro 2004 Portugal logo.

Cristiano Ronaldo.description: Appears in a black Manchester United training top and later in a red match jersey with 'vodafone' sponsorship, actively scoring goals, celebrating, and displaying focused expressions during the 2004-2005 season. He is frequently named by the narrator and an excited match commentator during a goal, who highlights his 'goal-scoring attributes,' 'great performance,' speed against 'Arsenal's solid right-back Lauren,' his role playing on the left, being a 'victim of some rash tackles,' and successfully converting a penalty.

Archival Football Match Footage.description: Grainy, low-resolution clips from 2004-2005 matches (Fulham, Blackburn, Bolton, AC Milan, Newcastle) showing Ronaldo dribbling, shooting, and scoring, accompanied by crowd cheering and a male narrator's commentary on the action and match outcomes.

Quote Text 'HE’S A GOOD KID, BUT SOMEONE.description: White text overlaid on a red grid background, attributed to Luiz Scolari, highlighting early skepticism about Ronaldo’s confidence, and narrated by a clear, slightly accented male voice.
