raw_segments: The segment opens with a dramatic, slightly accented male narrator quoting Luiz Scolari, stating, 'As Scolari said, that he's a good kid, but someone must have told him he's the best in the world. And if he thinks he is, it will be very hard to work with him.' This quote is displayed in bold white text against a dark red grid background, accompanied by Scolari’s portrait. Epic, dramatic background music begins as the scene transitions to a montage of Cristiano Ronaldo in his Portugal national team jersey (number 17, red with green trim, Nike logo, and a yellow armband). The narrator explains that 'Cristiano was gaining more minutes as the tournament progressed.' Flags of Greece, Russia, and Spain appear sequentially, with the narrator detailing match results: Portugal 1-2 Greece, where Ronaldo entered in the second half, gave away a penalty, and scored, but Portugal lost; Russia 0-2 Portugal, where Ronaldo assisted; and Spain 0-1 Portugal, where Ronaldo started and was named man of the match. Brief clips of match action show Ronaldo celebrating, frustrated, or intense. The sequence continues with Portugal 2-1 England (penalty shootout 6-5), where Ronaldo started the quarter-final, and visually, Portugal 2-1 Ukraine, though the narrator mentions a win against the Netherlands in the semi-finals where Ronaldo assisted and scored, with Ronaldo shown shirtless in celebration. A black-and-white team photo of the Portugal squad is shown, with Ronaldo in the front row, as the narrator states he became the youngest player ever to start a final. The segment ends with a clip of the 0-1 loss to Greece, where Ronaldo is seen dejected and screaming in frustration, as the narrator concludes that the final ended like the opener, with Greece winning and Ronaldo missing a huge chance and being very upset. The dramatic music swells throughout.

The segment opens with a dramatic black-and-white image of Cristiano Ronaldo screaming in frustration during a Euro 2004 match, a '0-1' score and Greek flag hinting at a loss, set against a somber, dramatic background music. A clear, authoritative male narrator begins, explaining that "Cristiano came back to United a different person." The scene transitions to a close-up of a younger, confident Ronaldo in a Manchester United training top (number 10). The narrator then quotes Mike Phelan, whose words are simultaneously displayed as white text on a dark red background: "His body shape was different, his facial features were different... That’s an athletic physique for the British game." Footage of Ronaldo in a red Manchester United jersey with 'vodafone' sponsorship, running during training, showcases his athleticism. The narrator continues, noting Ronaldo's performances remained inconsistent, which weighed on Gary Neville, whose quote is then displayed and spoken by the narrator: "Do you live with him for another 12 months? Where he’s doing OK but not scoring 25 goals? Where he can turn off and not defend?" This is intercut with grainy archival match footage from 2004-2005 against Fulham, Blackburn, and Bolton, where Ronaldo is seen dribbling, shooting, and scoring, with on-screen graphics indicating match details. The narrator describes Ronaldo's continued focus on "new skills" despite "wrong decisions" but also his "slight improvements" in passing. The segment concludes with the narrator stating Ronaldo "now has a rocket of a shot," immediately followed by an excited male commentator exclaiming "Now he's got!" and a crowd cheer, reinforcing the dynamic play seen in the match highlights.

The video opens with a montage of archival football match highlights from the 2004-2005 season, presented on a retro-style screen with a red border, showcasing Cristiano Ronaldo in action for Manchester United against Bolton. As Ronaldo scores, a male narrator exclaims, "the goal! And what a goal!" amidst crowd cheering. More highlights show Ronaldo's dynamic play against Fulham, Blackburn, and Bolton, with on-screen graphics detailing match dates and scores. The narrator comments on Ronaldo's skill, noting, "And on his weak foot as well," and describes a near-miss, "A shot! Oh, the crossbar saves Bolton." Close-ups of Ronaldo in his red Manchester United jersey display intense focus and determination, as the narrator explains his physical development: "His physical endurance started to last the whole match, and he started to successfully overcome the defenders' challenges." A dramatic, slightly melancholic instrumental background music begins as the narrator continues to reflect on Ronaldo's progress, stating, "The improvement was obvious, but he was still one or two years away from delivering on a very high level." The scene transitions to a still image of Sir Alex Ferguson, Carlos Queiroz, and Wayne Rooney, all smiling as Rooney holds up a Manchester United jersey with 'ROONEY 8' on the back, symbolizing a key signing. The narrator highlights these events, mentioning "the return of the assistant Carlos Queiroz and the signing of Golden Boy Wayne Rooney." The narrative then shifts to a portrait of José Mourinho in a blue-and-white Chelsea training top, set against the club’s crest, as the narrator explains the competitive landscape: "United was still far away from competing, especially with the arrival of Jose at Chelsea and the huge spending that happened there." The EPL 04/05 standings are displayed, highlighting United’s third-place finish with 77 points, while the narrator confirms, "As the story of last year was repeated, and United ended the season in third position, 18 points away from the champions Chelsea." The video then cuts to a side-by-side image of Ferguson and Arsène Wenger, followed by match highlights from the 2005 Champions League Round of 16 against AC Milan, with an on-screen graphic showing 'MILAN 1(2-0)0 UNITED MAR 8, 2005'. The narrator recounts this setback: "That was followed by an early exit in Europe against Milan in the round of 16 of the Champions League, which was a tough match for Cristiano." A brief, intense crowd roar and a distinct goal sound effect punctuate the narrative. The video then shows FA Cup semifinal highlights against Newcastle, where United triumphed 4-1, indicated by an on-screen graphic '2005 FA CUP SEMIFINALS N'CASTLE 1-4 UNITED'. The segment concludes with a close-up of Ronaldo celebrating a goal, his face beaming with joy, as the crowd roars in the background, capturing the peak of his early success at United. The narrator notes, "But the team managed to get to the FA Cup final again. This time United faced Arsenal in the final, the team that was defeated both home and away."

The segment opens with a close-up of Cristiano Ronaldo in a black Manchester United training top, smiling and interacting with teammates, as a narrator introduces 'the Red Devils' and highlights Ronaldo's 'goal-scoring attributes.' The scene cuts to a dynamic match where Ronaldo scores a goal, met with an excited match commentator shouting 'Ronaldo! Chance Manchester United! Goal! Manchester United! Cristiano Ronaldo!' amidst crowd cheers and a whistle. A scoreboard graphic displays '04/05 PREMIER LEAGUE - GW25 ARSENAL 2-4 UNITED RONALDO - 54' 58''. Ronaldo celebrates with teammates, including a hug with Giggs, before the narrator shifts focus to the 'FA Cup Final,' where Ronaldo is seen with a focused expression. The narrator describes Ronaldo's 'great performance,' being 'one step faster than Arsenal's solid right-back Lauren' while playing on the left, and being the 'number one victim of some rash tackles.' Footage shows Ronaldo dribbling past an Arsenal defender and scoring, sending the goalkeeper sprawling, followed by his celebration. Match commentary notes 'frustration, despair' from the opposition. A brief shot of Sir Alex Ferguson and Roy Keane on the sidelines is shown as the narrator explains the final was 'settled through penalties' and Ronaldo 'successfully converted his penalty,' though United 'failed to retain the cup' and ended the season 'without a trophy.' The segment concludes with a graphic summarizing Ronaldo’s 2004-2005 season stats (33 PL apps, 5 goals, 4 assists; 8 CL apps, 0 goals, 2 assists; 7 FA Cup apps, 4 goals, 3 assists; 2 EFL Cup apps, 0 goals, 0 assists) with 'AGE: 20' displayed, as the narrator discusses 'Cristiano's numbers for the season' and his 'great performances within the FA Cup.' Throughout, background music plays, accompanied by match sounds and crowd noise.

event_blocks.summary: A graphic displaying Cristiano Ronaldo's 2004-2005 Manchester United season statistics at age 20, showing 33 Premier League appearances with 5 goals and 4 assists, 8 Champions League appearances with 0 goals and 2 assists, 7 FA Cup appearances with 4 goals and 3 assists, and 2 EFL Cup appearances with 0 goals and 0 assists, precedes a narrator's observation that Ronaldo was "yet to be one of the team's most important players," experiencing "a dip in form and inconsistent performances" that raised "questions about how to use him correctly." Ronaldo is depicted in a black training top and a red Manchester United match jersey, exhibiting focus and celebration, followed by a stylized graphic. On the training ground, Ronaldo, in a grey top, is addressed by coaches, including Carlos Queiroz, identifiable by his 'CQ' jacket. Queiroz's voice is heard predicting, "Over time, and when he's 28 years of age or even 29 years of age, I'm sure that Cristiano will convert into a pure striker." The scene transitions to Ronaldo in a blue training top, then exiting a black car amid fans. The narrator then announces the "tragic news of the passing of his father on September of 2005." A quote from Maria Dolores is presented, with the narrator relaying her statement that "Cristiano's father drank until his death, and that destroyed Ronaldo," adding that "drugs and alcohol were a main stay in Madeira's hoods." The segment concludes with a dramatic wave animation and a dimly lit family scene featuring a man in a plaid shirt holding a baby with a woman in a white dress, as the narrator mentions Cristiano assisting his brother Hugo.

event_blocks.summary: The narrative begins with a somber reflection on drug abuse, quickly shifting to intense close-ups of Cristiano Ronaldo in a red Manchester United jersey, his focused and emotional expressions during matches juxtaposed with personal struggles that season, including a red card for a dangerous challenge and an obscene gesture towards Benfica fans, as confirmed by a referee's action and a crowd roar. A black-and-white newspaper headline announces Ronaldo's arrest over rape allegations, with the narrator confirming the sexual assault accusation. The video then returns to match action, showing Ronaldo dribbling and scoring, followed by a crowd celebration and stadium atmosphere. A stylized montage features Ronaldo celebrating with teammates like David Beckham against a red grid background, displaying his goal statistics ('44' for 02-03 and '16' for 04-05). The narrator explains how Ronaldo's showboating and delayed crosses strained his relationship with striker Ruud van Nistelrooy, whose goal numbers suffered, contrasting Beckham's precise crosses with Ronaldo's individual play. The segment concludes with Ronaldo in a blue training top during a training session, interacting with teammates, as the narrator details the complicated relationship between 'the Portuguese' and 'the Dutchman,' mentioning injuries. Gary Neville expresses doubts about Ronaldo's performance in his mid-third year, questioning if he could become a top player. Archival match footage from late 2005 and early 2006 shows Ronaldo in his red Manchester United jersey, dribbling, shooting, and scoring against teams like Debrecen, Newcastle, Benfica, and Chelsea, with commentary noting his 'physical development' and 'great ability in counter-attacks.' A match against Fulham on February 4, 2006, sees Ronaldo score a free kick with his signature knuckleball technique, described as an 'absolute belter,' contributing to a 2-0 victory. The sequence ends with Ronaldo celebrating his first European goal against Debrecen in the Champions League qualifying phase, with the scoreline 'UNITED 3-0 DEBRECEN' and his goal time '63’' displayed.

raw_segments: The video opens with two men walking along a paved path in a green, park-like setting, as a male narrator gravely recounts a story about failing to save someone from drug abuse. The scene quickly shifts to intense close-ups of Cristiano Ronaldo in a red Manchester United 'vodafone' jersey, displaying focused and emotional expressions during matches, while the narrator connects these to personal issues affecting him that season, detailing his sending off for a dangerous challenge and an obscene gesture aimed at Benfica fans. A dramatic moment follows as a referee shows Ronaldo a red card, accompanied by a crowd roar and a whistle. The narrative then transitions to a black-and-white newspaper headline announcing Ronaldo's arrest over rape allegations, with the narrator confirming his accusation of sexual assault. The video returns to football action, showing Ronaldo dribbling and scoring in his red jersey, followed by a crowd celebration and stadium atmosphere. A stylized montage features Ronaldo celebrating with teammates, including David Beckham, against a red grid background, while on-screen graphics display his goal statistics: '44' for 02-03 and '16' for 04-05. The narrator explains how Ronaldo's insistence on showing off skills and delaying crosses affected his relationship with 'the main striker of the team,' Ruud van Nistelrooy, and how Van Nistelrooy's goal numbers took a 'huge hit,' contrasting Beckham's pinpoint crosses with Ronaldo's individual play. The segment concludes with Ronaldo in a blue training top, interacting with teammates during a training session, as the narrator continues to detail the complicated relationship between 'the Portuguese' and 'the Dutchman,' mentioning injuries as a factor.

The minute opens with Manchester United players, including Cristiano Ronaldo, Gary Neville, and Wayne Rooney, in blue training tops with 'vodafone' sponsorship, on a grassy field. A male narrator immediately sets the scene, noting that "Cristiano's settling period took a very long time." The visual then transitions to a dark red graphic displaying a quote from Gary Neville, which the narrator voices, stating Neville's doubts: "I questioned Ronaldo's performance in his mid-third year. I was getting to the point where I wondered whether he could actually be a top player." The narrator then invites viewers to "watch some clips from that season," as the video shifts to a montage of grainy archival match footage from late 2005 and early 2006. This dynamic sequence showcases Ronaldo in a red Manchester United match jersey, dribbling, shooting, and scoring against various teams like Debrecen, Newcastle, Benfica, and Chelsea. During these highlights, football commentary and crowd noise emerge, with one commentator exclaiming, "And here goes old twinkle toes again!" The narrator continues, highlighting Ronaldo's "physical development" and "great ability in counter-attacks." More match action follows with commentary like "Ronaldo... slip." On-screen text introduces a match: 'UNITED - FULHAM FEB 4, 2006'. Ronaldo scores, leading to a celebration with the text 'UNITED 2-0 FULHAM RONALDO - 14’' displayed, accompanied by excited commentary: "Ronaldo, that's better, perhaps a whole lot better! It's an absolute belter!" The narrator adds that "for the first time, Cristiano successfully scoring a free kick with the same knuckleball technique that he uses today." The sequence concludes with Ronaldo celebrating another goal against Debrecen in the Champions League qualifying phase, with the scoreline 'UNITED 3-0 DEBRECEN' and his goal time '63’' on screen, as the narrator mentions he "managed to score his first European goal."

event_blocks.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo, clad in a Manchester United jersey, jubilantly celebrates his first Champions League goal against Debrecen, marked by the on-screen text 'UNITED 3-0 DEBRECEN RONALDO - 63’'. Despite this, Manchester United faced Champions League group stage elimination for the third consecutive year, a struggle mirrored by Sir Alex Ferguson's animated sideline gestures and a Group D standings graphic showing United level with Lille. The narrative then pivots to league performance, highlighting Chelsea's Premier League triumph with an eight-point lead over United, as evidenced by the 2005-06 Premier League standings graphic. With an early FA Cup exit, signified by a blacked-out FA Cup logo alongside an active EFL Cup logo, the League Cup represented United's final title opportunity. Ferguson, observing a tactical formation graphic displaying a 4-4-2 with Ronaldo on the right wing, opted to start Saha, Ronaldo, and Rooney in the League Cup final. Ronaldo subsequently scored in the final against Wigan, confirmed by 'UNITED 4-0 WIGAN FEB 26, 2006', and added another goal in the decisive 4-0 victory, culminating in a shirtless celebration with a red scarf, a moment a commentator described as one to 'treasure for the rest of his life'. A detailed stats graphic for Ronaldo's 2005-06 season, showing him at age 21, accompanied the narrator's analysis of his developing performance, which Ferguson viewed as a 'year of development' despite fan skepticism, as Ferguson walked composedly on the pitch amidst the crowd's roar.

raw_segments: The segment opens with Cristiano Ronaldo, in a red Manchester United jersey, dynamically celebrating his first ever European goal against Debrecen in the Champions League qualifiers, confirmed by the on-screen text 'UNITED 3-0 DEBRECEN RONALDO - 63’'. The narrator explains that for the third consecutive year, United were struggling and crashed out of the Champions League group stages, a struggle visually underscored by Sir Alex Ferguson animatedly gesturing on the sidelines, followed by a graphic of Group D standings showing Manchester United tied with Lille. The narrative shifts to the league, with a vintage clip of a Chelsea player scoring, as the narrator details Chelsea's dominance, lifting the Premier League with an eight-point gap over United, a fact reinforced by the 2005-06 Premier League standings graphic showing Chelsea leading. Ferguson reappears beside a blacked-out FA Cup logo and an active EFL Cup logo, signifying United's early FA Cup exit and their sole remaining chance for a title in the League Cup. Ronaldo and teammates are then seen looking intense on the bench. A tactical formation graphic, showing a 4-4-2 setup with Ronaldo on the right wing, accompanies the narrator's explanation of Ferguson's decision to start Saha, Ronaldo, and Rooney in the League Cup final. The video then shows Ronaldo scoring in the 2006 League Cup Final against Wigan, with the text 'UNITED 4-0 WIGAN FEB 26, 2006' displayed, followed by a shirtless Ronaldo celebrating with a red scarf, as the narrator confirms he added another goal in United's handsome victory. A commentator briefly interjects, stating the goal 'will treasure for the rest of his life'. The segment concludes with a detailed stats graphic for Ronaldo’s 2005-06 season, noting his age 21, as the narrator analyzes his numbers, suggesting slight improvements in goals, and summarizing the season as a 'year of development' from Ferguson's perspective, though fans were not as convinced, while Ferguson walks composedly on the pitch amidst crowd roars.

event_blocks.summary: The summer of 2006 saw significant player movement, with Chelsea Football Club initiating a spending spree that brought Shevchenko for €43.88M, Kalou for €4.00M, Ballack on a free transfer, and Ashley Cole for €7.40M. Concurrently, Manchester United saw Van Nistelrooy depart for Real Madrid and Roy Keane leave for Celtic after issues with the manager, while Sir Alex Ferguson welcomed Michael Carrick from Spurs, assigning him the number 16 jersey. Despite a challenging period for United, marked by three consecutive seasons without a league title, Ferguson expressed confidence in his young core to improve. The focus then shifted to the World Cup 2006, where Cristiano Ronaldo joined the Portugal squad, scoring against Iran in the group stage. Footage captured Ronaldo celebrating a goal against Ireland (2-0), with a commentator noting his origin. However, Ronaldo sustained an injury during the Round of 16 match against the Netherlands, the 'Battle of Nuremberg,' leading to his substitution. Despite these concerns, Ronaldo recovered swiftly and started in the quarterfinals match against England on July 1, 2006, appearing determined in a determined Portugal jersey.

Chelsea Football Club Crest.description: A blue circular crest with a rampant lion holding a staff, surrounded by footballs and flowers, set against a dark grid background, representing the club's 'continued spending spree' as described by the narrator.

Portugal National Team.description: A group photo of the 2006 World Cup squad in maroon jerseys with green trim, including Ronaldo (number 17), Figo (number 7), and others, posing on the pitch, representing the squad Cristiano joined for the World Cup as narrated.

Van Nistelrooy.description: A man in a suit holding a white Real Madrid jersey with 'VANNISTELROOY' on the back, against a red grid background, whose transfer to Real Madrid from United is narrated.

Michael Carrick.description: A young man in a red Manchester United training jersey with number 37, standing beside Ferguson, holding the 'CARRICK 16' jersey, whose arrival from Spurs is announced by the narrator.

Ferguson (Timeline Portrait).description: A close-up of Sir Alex Ferguson in a black coat and glasses, with a timeline above him showing '03/04', '04/05', and '05/06', representing the period where, as narrated, he remained confident despite United's three consecutive seasons of failing in the league.

Cristiano Ronaldo (World Cup).description: A young Ronaldo in a maroon Portugal jersey with number 17, celebrating a goal with arms raised, then sitting injured on the pitch, and later looking focused during the England match, with on-screen text indicating match details. The narrator details his World Cup journey, scoring against Iran, being injured and subbed during the 'Battle of Nuremberg' against the Dutch, and then recovering quickly to start against England. His goal celebration is accompanied by crowd cheering and a commentator's excited shout, 'And the boy from Madeira!'

World Cup Match Graphics.description: On-screen text overlays indicating match results and times: 'POR 2-0 IRA RONALDO - 80’', 'POR 0-0 NED RONALDO INJURY - 34’', and 'ENG - POR JULY 1, 2006', visually supporting the narrated events of Ronaldo's goal, injury, and subsequent quarterfinal match.

World Cup 2006 Title Card.description: Bold, glowing pink text reading 'WORLD CUP SUMMER 2006' on a black background, introducing the international tournament segment, with the narrator stating 'Cristiano joined the Portugal squad for the World Cup'.

Sir Alex Ferguson.description: An older man in a white shirt with 'AF' on the chest, holding a red Manchester United jersey with 'CARRICK 16' with Michael Carrick, against a red grid background. He is later seen in a close-up portrait with a timeline, described by the narrator as confident despite United's recent league failures, expecting his young core to step up.

Ballack.description: A headshot of Michael Ballack in a blue Chelsea jersey, labeled as a free transfer, whose name is spoken by the narrator as part of Chelsea's summer transfers.

Shevchenko.description: A headshot of Andriy Shevchenko in a blue Chelsea jersey, labeled with a transfer fee of €43.88M, whose name is spoken by the narrator as part of Chelsea's summer transfers.

Kalou.description: A headshot of Salomon Kalou in a blue Chelsea jersey, labeled with a transfer fee of €4.00M, whose name is spoken by the narrator as part of Chelsea's summer transfers.

Portugal vs Ireland Match Footage.description: Action footage from the 2006 World Cup Group D match, showing Ronaldo scoring a goal and celebrating, with stadium and advertising boards visible in the background, accompanied by crowd cheering and a commentator's excited call.

A. Cole.description: A headshot of Ashley Cole in a blue Chelsea jersey, labeled with a transfer fee of €7.40M, whose name is spoken by the narrator as part of Chelsea's summer transfers.

England vs Portugal Quarterfinals Title.description: White text on a dark background reading '2006 WC - QUARTERFINALS ENG - POR JULY 1, 2006', indicating the upcoming match, which the narrator confirms Ronaldo recovered quickly to start.

Keane.description: A man in a suit holding a green-and-white Celtic jersey with 'KEANE' and number 6, against a red grid background, whose departure from United to Celtic is narrated, citing 'issues with the manager'.

Portugal vs Netherlands Match Footage.description: Footage of Ronaldo being tackled and injured during the Round of 16 match, with medical staff attending to him on the pitch, illustrating the narrated 'Battle of Nuremberg' injury.

raw_segments: The video opens with a dark, grid-patterned background featuring the Chelsea Football Club crest, transitioning to a graphic titled 'SUMMER 2006' listing transfers. A narrator details Chelsea's "continued spending spree" as Shevchenko (€43.88M), Kalou (€4.00M), Ballack (FREE), and Ashley Cole (€7.40M) all joined in the summer of 2006. The scene shifts to a red-toned backdrop with two men holding jerseys, as the narrator explains that Manchester United sold Van Nistelrooy to Real Madrid and Roy Keane left for Celtic in November of the previous season "after some issues with the manager." This is followed by Sir Alex Ferguson and Michael Carrick holding a Manchester United jersey with 'CARRICK 16', with the narrator introducing Carrick's arrival from Spurs. A timeline graphic appears with '03/04', '04/05', and '05/06' above Ferguson’s portrait, as the narrator describes it as a "tough summer for United," but Ferguson remained "confident" despite "failing in the league for three consecutive seasons," expecting the "young core" to "step up quickly." The atmosphere shifts from club transfer news to international football drama, introduced by a 'WORLD CUP SUMMER 2006' title card. The narrator states that "Cristiano joined the Portugal squad for the World Cup," playing in two group stage matches and scoring against the Iranian national team. This is visually supported by Portugal’s team photo, followed by footage of Ronaldo celebrating a goal against Ireland (POR 2-0 IRA, 80’), accompanied by crowd cheering and a commentator exclaiming, "And the boy from Madeira!" The narrative then moves to his injury during the Round of 16 match against the Netherlands (POR 0-0 NED, 34’), with the narrator explaining he was "subbed because of injury" during the "Battle of Nuremberg." The final frames show Ronaldo in a Portugal jersey, looking determined, with on-screen text indicating the quarterfinals match against England on July 1, 2006, as the narrator concludes that despite initial fears, Ronaldo "recovered quickly" and was a starter.

event_blocks.summary: The 2006 World Cup quarterfinals saw Wayne Rooney receive a red card for a high tackle on a Portuguese player, leading to Cristiano Ronaldo's visible frustration and a subsequent wink to the Portuguese bench, which the media later interpreted as a sign of his involvement in Rooney's dismissal. Portugal ultimately won the penalty shootout, with Ronaldo scoring the decisive penalty. Post-match, Ronaldo faced intense media scrutiny in England, being labeled the 'number one villain,' which led him to consider leaving Manchester United. However, advised by his agent Mendes and reassured by Sir Alex Ferguson, who drew parallels to David Beckham's 1998 experience, Ronaldo decided to stay. The 2006-2007 season commenced with a strong sense of unity within Manchester United, despite external media speculation about rifts between Ronaldo and Rooney. Ronaldo's performance rapidly improved, marked by a goal in the Premier League opener against Fulham and consistent effectiveness throughout the season, earning him back-to-back Barclays Player of the Month awards in November and December 2006. He demonstrated exceptional goal-scoring form, netting braces in three consecutive matches and scoring crucial late winners, such as against Fulham. Manchester United led the EPL standings, maintaining a significant gap from Chelsea. Ronaldo's impact extended to the Champions League, where he scored his first goals in the competition proper against Roma in the quarterfinals, contributing significantly to United's victories in both the quarterfinals and the semifinals against Milan, despite a challenging second leg at the San Siro.

Cristiano Ronaldo.description: Wearing a red Manchester United jersey with 'AIG' sponsorship and the club crest, appearing focused, skillful, and celebratory after scoring goals in the 06/07 Premier League matches against Reading and Fulham, and later in a white away kit during the 06/07 Champions League semifinal against Milan. The narrator frequently refers to him as 'Cristiano' or 'Ronaldo', detailing his outstanding performances, contract extension until 2012 with a weekly wage of 119,000 pounds, scoring a brace against Roma, and struggling in the San Siro against Milan. A commentator also excitedly announces his 'first goal in the Champions League proper!' amidst crowd roars.

Newspaper Headline.description: A black-and-white graphic with bold text 'Real boss Capello keen on Ronaldo,' symbolizing media speculation about Ronaldo’s future, which the narrator confirms was fueled by an open transfer window and media narratives.

Quote Text.description: White text on a dark red grid background, attributed to Cristiano Ronaldo, stating: 'They told me to push further forward to make the most of the numerical advantage and that’s how I responded.' The audio directly references this, with Cristiano talking about the wink and saying 'it was a reaction after receiving some instructions from the coaching staff to push forward and make most of the numerical advantage'.

Sven-Göran Eriksson.description: England’s manager, wearing a white shirt with a green lanyard, seen walking off the pitch with a hand to his face, looking distressed.

Crowd Celebration.description: Close-up of Portuguese fans in red and green, cheering wildly and waving flags, their excitement amplified by the audio of loud crowd noise and a commentator exclaiming 'Portugal are World Cup semi-finalists!'

Portugal National Team Jersey.description: Maroon with green trim and number 17, worn by Ronaldo during the match and in the graphic, featuring the Portuguese crest and Nike logo.

Referee.description: Wearing a yellow shirt and black shorts, shown raising a red card during a match, indicating a disciplinary action.

Scoreboard Graphic.description: On-screen text displaying '2006 WC - QUARTERFINALS ENG 0(1-3)0 POR FULL TIME,' indicating the final result of the match after penalties, which the audio confirms as Portugal winning after 'Cristiano scored the match deciding penalty'.

Medical Staff.description: A figure in a white shirt carrying a medical bag, seen walking past Ronaldo, indicating the aftermath of the red card incident.

England National Team Jersey.description: White with red accents and number 9, worn by Rooney during the match, featuring the England crest and Umbro logo.

Wayne Rooney.description: Wearing a black Manchester United training top with the number 9, seen standing with Ronaldo in the rain, and mentioned by the narrator as being heavily scrutinized by the media during the Fulham match, with false reports of a strained relationship with Ronaldo.

Penalty Kick Scene.description: A wide-angle shot of the penalty area with the goalkeeper diving to his right as the ball enters the net. The audio identifies Portugal's keeper as 'Ricardo' who 'successfully saved three attempts,' and confirms Cristiano scored the decisive penalty.

Man in Suit and Sunglasses.description: A man in a dark suit, wearing sunglasses, holding a phone to his ear, appearing serious and contemplative. The narrator identifies him as Mendes, stating Cristiano asked him about leaving, but Mendes advised him to go back to Manchester, and he later sat with Ferguson and Cristiano.

Zinedine Zidane.description: Wearing a white France jersey with number 10 and red-blue trim, celebrating a goal with a clenched fist, with on-screen text indicating the 2006 World Cup semifinal goal against Portugal.

Sir Alex Ferguson.description: Wearing a black coat, celebrating with arms raised in triumph, surrounded by staff and fans in a stadium setting, capturing the electric atmosphere of a major victory. His celebration is seen as the narrator speaks of a 'young player turning into a real star'.

Manchester United Jersey.description: Worn by Ronaldo in red during the Fulham match, featuring 'AIG' sponsorship and the club crest, and later in white during the Charlton match, with the same branding and crest, visible during goal celebrations and on-screen stats. The narrator refers to the team as 'United' and mentions their six-point gap from Chelsea and their high-level performance in the Champions League.

Collage of Smiling Faces.description: A red grid background with white-framed portraits of smiling Ferguson, Gary Neville, and Ronaldo, visually reinforcing the team unity that the narrator describes Ferguson as cultivating among the players.

Title Card 'UNITED 2006 - 2007'.description: A glowing red title card introducing the 2006-2007 season, which the narrator details as a period of intense media scrutiny and team building for Manchester United.

On-Screen Text.description: Displays match details such as 'UNITED 5-1 FULHAM FULL TIME', '06/07 PREMIER LEAGUE - GW1', 'CHARLTON - UNITED AUG 23, 2006', and the quote from Gary Neville, providing context and narrative progression.

Saha.description: Wearing a red Manchester United jersey with 'SAHA 9' on the back, seen celebrating with Ronaldo after the goal against Fulham, appearing joyful and animated.

Goalkeeper.description: Wearing a green goalkeeper kit, seen diving to his right in an attempt to save Ronaldo’s goal against Fulham, but failing as the ball enters the net.

Gary Neville.description: Visually shown embracing a teammate with his quote 'MOST ENJOYABLE 6 MONTHS THAT I’VE SEEN AT UNITED'. Audibly, the narrator mentions him just before his quote appears, setting up the context of his statement about United's strong start.

Didier Drogba.description: Appearing in a blue Chelsea jersey in a split-screen graphic, symbolizing a rivalry with Ronaldo. Audibly, the narrator mentions 'Chelsea' in the context of the title race, which Drogba visually represents.

Title Card 'VILLA - UNITED DEC 23, 2006'.description: White text on a black background, displayed on the retro-style screen, introducing a match highlight. Audibly, it introduces a highlight where a commentator shouts 'Second bite! Breakthrough!'

Title Card 'READING - UNITED SEP 23, 200.description: White text on a black background, displayed on the retro-style screen, introducing a match highlight. Audibly, it introduces a highlight where a commentator notes Ronaldo 'threatened every time he's had the ball.'

Title Card 'UNITED - EVERTON DEC 23, 200.description: White text on a black background, displayed on the retro-style screen, introducing a match highlight. Audibly, it introduces a highlight where a commentator shouts 'Ronaldo! Gooooal!'

Title Card 'UNITED - WATFORD JAN 31, 200.description: White text on a black background, displayed on a retro-style screen with a red border, introducing a match highlight. Audibly, it introduces a highlight where a commentator praises Ronaldo's 'great little ball.'

Archival Football Match Footage.description: Grainy, low-resolution clips from 2006-2007 matches (Reading, Fulham) showing Ronaldo dribbling, shooting, and scoring, accompanied by excited commentator exclamations like 'And he finds the net with a brilliant shot!' and 'Ronaldo goes! And he scores the goal!' with crowd roars.

Title Card 'UNITED - SHEFFIELD UNITED MA.description: White text on a black background, displayed on the same retro-style screen, introducing a match highlight. Audibly, it introduces a highlight where a commentator praises Ronaldo's 'lovely touch.'

Title Card 'BENFICA - UNITED SEP 26, 200.description: White text on a black background, displayed on the retro-style screen, introducing a match highlight. Audibly, it introduces a highlight with a Spanish commentator praising Ronaldo's 'fuerza y velocidad.'

EPL 06/07 Standings Graphic.description: A red grid background with white text listing the top four teams: United (69), Chelsea (63), Arsenal (55), and Liverpool (53), with United highlighted in a white bar, emphasizing their league leadership, visually confirming the narrator's statement about United maintaining a six-point gap from Chelsea.

Title Card 'UNITED - CELTIC APR 17, 2007.description: White text on a black background, displayed on the retro-style screen, introducing a match highlight. Audibly, it introduces a highlight where a commentator shouts 'Cristiano Ronaldo!'

Robbie Fowler.description: Shown in a red Liverpool jersey with 'Carlsberg' sponsorship, labeled 'FOWLER' and dated 'DEC '95 - JAN '96', mentioned by a narrator as a previous Player of the Month recipient in '95-'96.

Aston Villa FC Crest.description: A shield with a golden lion rampant on a blue background, labeled 'AVFC', displayed with a Nike logo beneath, mentioned by a narrator as one of the clubs Ronaldo scored a brace against.

Reading Football Club Crest.description: A circular crest with a horse and football, labeled 'READING FOOTBALL CLUB EST. 1871', displayed with a Nike logo beneath, mentioned by a narrator as one of the clubs Ronaldo scored a brace against, and later seen in live match footage where he scores two goals.

Wigan Athletic Crest.description: A circular crest with a green tree and crown, labeled 'WIGAN ATHLETIC 1932', displayed with a Nike logo beneath, mentioned by a narrator as one of the clubs Ronaldo scored a brace against.

Barclays Player of the Month.description: A graphic award shown in split-screen format with Ronaldo’s images from November and December 2006, featuring a stylized soccer ball and the Barclays logo, explicitly mentioned by a narrator as 'back-to-back Player of the Month awards'.

Dennis Bergkamp.description: Shown in a red Arsenal jersey with 'JVC' sponsorship, labeled 'BERGKAMP' and dated 'AUG SEP '97', mentioned by a narrator as a previous Player of the Month recipient in '97.

Champions League Match Graphics.description: On-screen text overlays indicating match results and times: 'UNITED 7(8-3)1 ROMA RONALDO - 44' 49'' and 'UNITED 3-2 MILAN APR 24, 2007', followed by 'MILAN (2-3) UNITED MAY 2, 2007', providing context for Ronaldo’s goals in the 06/07 Champions League semifinals. These graphics align with the narrator's detailed account of United scoring seven goals against Roma and facing Milan in the semifinals, winning the first leg 3-2.

Ronaldo Celebration Pose.description: A stylized image of Ronaldo screaming in triumph against a dark red backdrop with the Manchester United crest, accompanied by a timeline from 07/08 to 11/12 and a salary figure of €119,000, emphasizing his peak performance and financial value. This visual directly corresponds to the narrator's mention of Cristiano's contract extension until 2012 with a weekly wage of 119,000 pounds.

Manchester United Crest.description: Displayed prominently above Ferguson’s portrait on the red side of the split-screen, symbolizing the club’s interest in Ronaldo, and later visible on Ronaldo’s jersey during celebrations. It also appears on a dark red backdrop behind a stylized image of Ronaldo, reinforcing the club's identity as the narrator discusses Cristiano's contract with 'the club'.

raw_segments: The segment opens with intense action from the 2006 World Cup quarterfinals, as Wayne Rooney, in a white England jersey, commits a high tackle on a Portuguese player. The audio describes this as a 'physical battle for the ball' after an 'eventless hour,' leading to the referee, in a yellow shirt, showing Rooney a red card at the 62nd minute. Cristiano Ronaldo, in a maroon Portugal jersey, reacts with visible frustration and gestures, with the audio noting he 'came to ask for his English colleague to get sent off.' As a commentator exclaims 'tempers are flaring on the pitch,' the cameras catch Ronaldo winking to the Portuguese bench. The match eventually goes to penalties, with the audio highlighting Portugal's keeper, Ricardo, successfully saving three attempts. A penalty kick is shown where Portugal scores, giving them a 1-0 lead, and the audio confirms 'Cristiano scored the match deciding penalty.' The scoreboard updates to 'ENG 0(1-3)0 POR' at full time, indicating a 3-1 penalty shootout win for Portugal, as loud crowd noise and a commentator exclaiming 'Portugal are World Cup semi-finalists!' accompany Portuguese fans cheering wildly. A dejected England manager, Sven-Göran Eriksson, walks off. The scene transitions to a graphic of Ronaldo, contemplative, with a quote explaining his wink as a reaction to coaching staff instructions to 'push further forward to make the most of the numerical advantage.' This is followed by a newspaper-style headline, 'UGLY FACE OF SPORT' and 'PRESSURE BUILDS ON RONALDO,' reflecting the audio's narrative that the media 'wanted a scapegoat' and Ronaldo became 'the number one villain in England.' The final frame shows a serious Ronaldo as Rooney walks past with a medical bag, underscoring the controversial aftermath.

The segment opens with a tense football match scene, showing Cristiano Ronaldo in a maroon Portugal jersey (number 17) with hands on hips, flanked by opponents in white jerseys, including Wayne Rooney (number 9) carrying a medical bag, as a narrator explains that Ronaldo, Ferdinand, and Rooney discussed the matter, with Rooney warning Cristiano about the intense scrutiny from English fans and media that would dissect the issue throughout the summer. This narrative of media pressure is visually reinforced by a black-and-white newspaper-style graphic with the headline 'PRESSURE BUILDS ON RONALDO', featuring a dejected Ronaldo. The scene transitions to a stylized red grid background with framed headshots of Ronaldo, a player with dreadlocks, and Rooney, before cutting to Zinedine Zidane in a white France jersey (number 10) celebrating a goal, with on-screen text confirming the 2006 World Cup semifinal where Portugal lost 0-1 to France, a match after which Cristiano was voted the third best young player. The narrator reveals that following the World Cup and the loud response from the English media, Cristiano seriously considered not returning to Manchester, despite constant communication with Rooney. A split-screen of Ronaldo and Rooney is shown, followed by a man in a dark suit and sunglasses (identified by the narrator as Mendes) on the right, while Ronaldo is on the left, as the narrator explains Cristiano asked Mendes about leaving, but Mendes advised him to return to Manchester. The scene expands to include Sir Alex Ferguson and another man (possibly a coach) on the left, with a glowing white outline of Germany in the center, as the narrator details Ferguson's meeting with Mendes and Cristiano at Carlos Queiroz's house, where he spoke about David Beckham facing similar animosity in 1998. Archival footage of an England player (number 12, likely Neville) receiving a red card from a referee raising the card further illustrates the intense on-field drama. The segment concludes with Sir Alex Ferguson's voice stating, 'YOU’RE ONE OF THE BRAVEST PLAYERS TO COME TO MANCHESTER, BUT WALKING AWAY ISN'T COURAGE,' overlaid on a close-up of Ronaldo in a yellow training vest, followed by Ronaldo's own quote: 'THERE’S NO REASON FOR ME TO LEAVE A CLUB THAT HAS ALWAYS SUPPORTED ME,' as the narrator mentions Cristiano talking to the press, all set against a dramatic, tense orchestral background score.

The minute opens with a deep, authoritative male voice quoting Cristiano Ronaldo's dramatic statement, "THERE'S NO REASON FOR ME TO LEAVE A CLUB THAT HAS ALWAYS SUPPORTED ME," displayed in bold white text against a dark red grid background. The narrator continues, explaining that Ronaldo was staying and ready for the challenge, as the scene transitions to Ronaldo in a black Manchester United training shirt with the AIG logo, standing beside Sir Alex Ferguson and another man, followed by Ronaldo walking outdoors in a blue tracksuit. A glowing red title card, 'UNITED 2006 - 2007,' appears, setting the timeline, while the narrator confirms, "Finally, Cristiano has arrived." Electronic, slightly eerie, suspenseful music with a rhythmic beat begins as a collage of smiling faces, including Ferguson, Gary Neville, and Ronaldo, emphasizes team camaraderie. The narrator explains that in early summer training sessions, Ferguson sat with Neville, Rooney, and Ronaldo, instantly feeling the team was united and creating a sense that the "entire world was against Manchester United." Visually, Ronaldo is seen in a yellow training vest conversing with Ferguson on a pitch, then in match footage from the 2006-2007 Premier League opener against Fulham, wearing a red Manchester United jersey. A man in a suit is briefly shown on the pitch, followed by Ronaldo in a black training top standing in the rain beside Wayne Rooney, both in AIG-branded gear. A newspaper headline, 'Real boss Capello keen on Ronaldo,' appears, aligning with the narrator's discussion of the opening match receiving immense media attention, with every step and reaction from Rooney and Cristiano analyzed, and media painting a false picture of them "not getting along." The narrator adds that the open transfer window fueled speculation, as the video concludes with Ronaldo scoring a powerful goal, celebrating with a raised arm, and a close-up highlighting his focused performance.

The video opens with a dynamic football match, as a narrator explains that Manchester United took Fulham apart. An excited commentator exclaims, "This is an unbelievable start for Manchester United, for Ronaldo!" as Cristiano Ronaldo, in a red Manchester United jersey with 'RONALDO 7', scores a goal against Fulham, with the goalkeeper diving in vain. The crowd erupts in cheers, and Ronaldo celebrates jubilantly with teammates, including Saha in a red 'SAHA 9' jersey. On-screen text confirms 'UNITED 5-1 FULHAM FULL TIME' from the '06/07 Premier League GW1'. The narrator then shifts to discussing the challenges of away matches, where "boos and insults accompanied each touch on the ball from Cristiano." The scene transitions to Ronaldo in a white Manchester United away jersey with 'AIG' sponsorship during a match against Charlton on August 23, 2006. A commentator describes his "attacking play" as he dribbles past defenders, showcasing his agility before scoring another goal, followed by a jubilant celebration with teammates amidst crowd noise. The narrator further explains Ronaldo's evolving play, noting that even without goals or assists, he made correct plays to ensure United won. The video then cuts to a split-screen graphic featuring Ronaldo in a red United jersey and Didier Drogba in a blue Chelsea jersey, symbolizing their rivalry, as the narrator mentions the media's attention shifting to "the exciting race for the title between United and Chelsea." This is followed by a quote from Gary Neville: 'MOST ENJOYABLE 6 MONTHS THAT I’VE SEEN AT UNITED', overlaid on an image of Ronaldo embracing a teammate, with the narrator introducing Gary Neville's perspective on United's strong start.

The minute opens with a close-up of Cristiano Ronaldo in a red Manchester United jersey, 'RONALDO 7' visible, embracing a teammate against a dark red background, as a calm male narrator, accompanied by subtle dramatic synth music, describes the start of the season as enjoyable with several players performing at a high level. This transitions to a split-screen montage of five United players, including Ronaldo, Park, Rooney, and Saha, mid-shout in their red AIG-sponsored jerseys, reinforcing the narrator's mention of players like Saha, Giggs, Rooney, Scholes, and Ronaldo. Sir Alex Ferguson is then seen clapping beside a large '15' graphic, symbolizing a milestone, as the narrator states United were league leaders with 15 wins after 19 matches. A league table graphic for 'EPL 06/07 – GW19' confirms United's top position with 47 points, ahead of Chelsea (45), Liverpool (34), and Arsenal (33), with the narrator noting Chelsea's persistence. Ronaldo appears in a rain-soaked close-up, smiling confidently, while the narrator explains how he brought all his attributes together for meaningful, winning football, emphasizing 'less artistry and more effectiveness.' The scene then shifts to a series of retro-style match highlights, each introduced by a title card (e.g., 'UNITED - WATFORD JAN 31, 2007'), showcasing Ronaldo's dynamic performances. As the narrator highlights 'The technique,' a commentator exclaims, 'Great little ball from Cristiano Ronaldo!' during a dribbling sequence. Another commentator praises a 'Lovely touch again from Ronaldo!' during the 'UNITED - SHEFFIELD UNITED MAR 17, 2007' highlight, as the narrator mentions 'Physical strength.' A Spanish commentator energetically praises Ronaldo's 'fuerza y velocidad' (strength and speed) during the 'BENFICA - UNITED SEP 26, 2006' clip, following the narrator's cue on 'The speed.' The narrator then introduces 'And the goal scoring,' leading to commentators shouting 'Ronaldo! Gooooal!' and 'Second bite! Breakthrough!' amidst intense crowd roars during highlights from 'UNITED - EVERTON DEC 23, 2006' and 'VILLA - UNITED DEC 23, 2006.' The sequence concludes with Ronaldo celebrating a goal, arms raised, in front of a cheering crowd during the 'READING - UNITED SEP 23, 2006' highlight, as the narrator states he started to decide matches on his own, and a commentator adds, 'He's threatened every time he's had the ball.'

The minute opens with a close-up of Cristiano Ronaldo in a white Manchester United away jersey, screaming in celebration as a commentator exclaims, "And he finds the net with a brilliant shot!" A narrator then explains how Ronaldo's talent exploded in November and December 2006, leading him to win back-to-back Barclays Player of the Month awards, a feat highlighted by a split-screen graphic and not achieved since Dennis Bergkamp in '97 and Robbie Fowler in '95-'96. The narrator continues, detailing Ronaldo's impressive streak of scoring a brace in three consecutive matches against Aston Villa, Wigan, and Reading, as the respective club crests appear. Live match footage then shows Ronaldo, in his red Manchester United jersey with 'AIG' sponsorship, scoring twice in a 3-2 victory over Reading in the 06/07 Premier League, with an excited commentator confirming, "third game running, Cristiano Ronaldo has scored two goals!" The narrative shifts to a crucial away match against Fulham on February 24, 2007, where, as the narrator recounts, United were in danger of dropping points at Craven Cottage. In the 88th minute, Ronaldo stepped up, deciding the match with a great solo effort. The footage captures his powerful run, shot, and jubilant celebration, with a commentator's electrifying call, "Ronaldo's away this time... he's three up with it... Ronaldo goes! And he scores the goal!" as the crowd roars. The scene concludes with Ronaldo in a focused side profile and a wide-angle view of the pitch, emphasizing the dramatic late winner.

The video opens with a jubilant Sir Alex Ferguson, in a black coat, celebrating with arms raised amidst staff and fans in a stadium, as a male narrator describes a young player turning into a real star under pressure from Chelsea. This transitions to Cristiano Ronaldo, in his red Manchester United jersey with 'AIG' sponsorship, celebrating a goal with intense emotion, fists clenched and mouth open in a roar, while the crowd erupts. A graphic then displays the EPL 06/07 standings at GW28, showing Manchester United leading with 69 points, followed by Chelsea, Arsenal, and Liverpool, visually reinforcing the narrator's mention of United maintaining a six-point gap from Chelsea. The narrative continues with a dramatic visual of Ronaldo screaming in triumph against a dark red backdrop with the Manchester United crest, accompanied by a timeline from 07/08 to 11/12 and a salary figure of €119,000, aligning with the narrator's detail of Cristiano's contract extension until 2012 with a weekly wage of 119,000 pounds. Ronaldo and teammates, including Smith (number 14), are then seen clapping the crowd in celebration. Action footage follows, showing Ronaldo scoring a goal against Roma in the 06/07 Champions League quarterfinals, with on-screen text marking his goals at 44' and 49', as the narrator explains Cristiano and United were performing on a high level, scoring seven goals against Roma, with Cristiano scoring a brace and assisting another. A commentator's excited shout of "Surely seals it!" and "His first goal in the Champions League proper!" accompanies the goal, confirming it was his first two goals in the Champions League. The sequence continues with Ronaldo scoring against Milan in the semifinals (3-2, 1st leg, April 24, 2007), followed by a close-up of him celebrating with a fierce expression, as the narrator details United facing Milan in the semifinals, winning the first leg 3-2. The final clip shows Ronaldo in a white away kit during the 2nd leg against Milan (2-3, May 2, 2007), with a focused look as he runs on the pitch, as the narrator mentions Cristiano struggling in the San Siro.

event_blocks.summary: In a Champions League semifinal, Cristiano Ronaldo, wearing a white Manchester United away kit, attempted a shot from the left but was tightly marked by AC Milan's Gennaro Gattuso, failing to influence the match as the ball narrowly missed the net, leading to United's 3-0 defeat and elimination from the competition, as confirmed by the scoreboard displaying 'Milan 3 (5-3) 0 United FULL TIME'. Later, Ronaldo, in a red Manchester United jersey, celebrated scoring a goal amidst crowd cheering and an excited Arabic commentator. The narrative shifted to the 2007 FA Cup Final between Chelsea and United, marked by on-screen text 'MAY 19, 2007', where Manchester United had secured their first Premier League title in four years. Ronaldo, in a red jersey, participated in the FA Cup Final against Mourinho's Chelsea, a match described as 'timid and mostly eventless', with Ronaldo starting on the left. The decisive moment came in extra time when Didier Drogba scored for Chelsea, accompanied by a commentator shouting "kicks!", with the scoreboard showing 'Chelsea 1 AET 0 United' and 'Drogba - 116’', and a commentator exclaiming "Drogba again! And Chelsea have scored!". event_blocks.summary: Amidst a jubilant crowd, Manchester United players, including Cristiano Ronaldo, celebrate lifting the Premier League trophy, a victory described as "so hard, but so, so sweet." The scene transitions to the 2008 Champions League Final between Manchester United and Chelsea, with tactical formations displayed. Cristiano Ronaldo, positioned on the left of the Manchester United jersey, was afforded freedom to exploit his speed against Chelsea's defense, though he faced tight marking from players like Michael Essien, Ballack, and Joe Cole. Ronaldo skillfully dribbled past a Chelsea defender and scored a powerful shot, confirmed by on-screen text as the opening goal in the 26th minute, leading to a massive crowd roar. Frank Lampard equalized for Chelsea in the 45th minute. The match progressed to a penalty shootout, where Cristiano Ronaldo's attempt was saved by Chelsea goalkeeper Petr Cech, a moment met with commentator surprise. The final moments show a visibly frustrated Ronaldo in the heavy rain after the missed penalty.

The narrator provides detailed analysis of the tactical setup, including Ronaldo's role and Chelsea's defensive strategy.

raw_segments: The minute opens with a celebratory scene of Manchester United players, including Cristiano Ronaldo, lifting the Premier League trophy adorned with a 'Barclays' ribbon, amidst a jubilant crowd, as a commentator remarks on the victory being "so hard, but so, so sweet." A calm, informative narrator with a non-native English accent then sets the scene for the 2008 Champions League Final between Manchester United and Chelsea, confirming the match details with on-screen text. Tactical formations for both teams are displayed, with the narrator detailing Ronaldo's role on the left side, given freedom to roam and exploit his speed against Chelsea's defense, which included Michael Essien as a right-back. The narrator continues to explain the tight marking on Cristiano by players like Essien, Ballack, and Joe Cole, but highlights his development in overcoming this. The action shifts to the pitch where Ronaldo, in his red 'AIG' Manchester United jersey, skillfully dribbles past a Chelsea defender and scores a powerful shot, leading to a massive crowd roar and an excited commentator shouting "Ronaldo!", with on-screen text confirming 'UNITED 1-0 CHELSEA RONALDO - 26''. Chelsea's Frank Lampard then equalizes in the 45th minute, as shown by the text 'UNITED 1-1 CHELSEA LAMPARD - 45'', a moment the narrator explicitly mentions. The narrator explains the match eventually went to a penalty shootout, and after two successful penalties for both sides, Cristiano stepped up to shoot. The video then shows Ronaldo attempting a penalty shot that is saved by Chelsea's goalkeeper, Petr Cech, prompting the excited commentator to exclaim, "And Cech has saved from Ronaldo of all people!" The final frames depict Ronaldo in the heavy rain, visibly frustrated and dejected after the missed penalty, with fans holding umbrellas in the background.

event_blocks.summary: Amidst a dramatic orchestral score and pouring rain, the 2008 Champions League Final unfolded with tension, culminating in Manchester United lifting the trophy after Van der Sar saved Anelka's penalty following Terry's slip. Cristiano Ronaldo, initially distraught in his rain-soaked Manchester United jersey after a missed opportunity, later celebrated jubilantly, lifting the Champions League trophy amidst confetti and cheers, embracing a smiling Sir Alex Ferguson. The narrator highlighted Ronaldo's potential Ballon d'Or win and his "double for Cristiano" and "another outstanding season," underscored by a stats graphic showcasing his 31 Premier League and 42 overall goals in 2007-2008, confirming his European Golden Boot award. Transitioning to a suspenseful electronic score, a title card appeared for 'EUROS AND THE 6TH SEASON 2007-2008'. Carlos Queiroz's past words to Ferguson were quoted: "if you manage to get five years out of Cristiano, then you've struck gold," noting "there is no precedent for a Portuguese teenager to go to another country and stay for five years." The segment concluded with Ronaldo and Ferguson, both medal-adorned, as hints of Ronaldo's departure emerged, referencing his quote, "I have said millions of times that I would love to play in Spain," revealing his pre-final dream of playing in Spain.

Quote Text 'I HAVE SAID MILLIONS OF TIME.description: White text overlaid on a red grid background, attributed to Cristiano Ronaldo, sourced from 'The Guardian'. The narrator references this sentiment, stating that Cristiano 'dreamed of playing in Spain' before the Champions League final.

Quote Text 'THERE’S NO PRECEDENT FOR IT'.description: White text overlaid on a red grid background, attributed to Carlos Queiroz, continuing the quote sequence. The narrator vocalizes this quote, elaborating on the unprecedented nature of a Portuguese teenager staying for five years in another country.

Champions League Trophy.description: A large silver trophy with a golden crown, held by Ronaldo and teammates during the celebration, with 'UEFA CHAMPIONS LEAGUE' inscribed on it. The narrator refers to it as 'the trophy' that United lifted and 'the title' that Cristiano lifted.

Carlos Queiroz.description: Wearing a dark Manchester United training jacket with the club crest, shown in a graphic with quotes about Ronaldo’s potential. The narrator directly quotes him stating, 'if you manage to get five years out of Cristiano, then you've struck gold,' and adding, 'There is no precedent for a Portuguese teenager to go to another country and stay for five years.'

Stats Graphic for Ronaldo 07-08.description: A red grid background with white text listing Ronaldo’s 2007-2008 season stats: 34 caps, 31 goals, 9 assists in the Premier League; 11 caps, 8 goals, 1 assist in the Champions League; 3 caps, 3 goals, 0 assists in the FA Cup; and 1 cap, 0 goals, 0 assists in the Community Shield, with 'AGE: 23' displayed beside his image. The narrator highlights his 31 league goals, 42 overall goals, and winning the European Golden Boot, directly correlating with the displayed statistics.

Quote Text 'IF YOU GET FIVE YEARS OUT OF.description: White text overlaid on a red grid background, attributed to Carlos Queiroz, sourced from 'Balague, Guillem. Cristiano Ronaldo: The Biography'. The narrator vocalizes this quote as part of Queiroz's past comments to Ferguson.

Cristiano Ronaldo.description: Wearing a red Manchester United jersey with 'AIG' sponsorship and the number 7, shown in various emotional states: distraught after a missed chance, celebrating with the Champions League trophy, and smiling beside Ferguson, with rain visibly soaking his jersey. The narrator frequently mentions him by name, detailing his achievements like lifting the trophy, potentially winning the Ballon d'Or, his 'double,' an 'outstanding season' with 31 league goals and 42 overall, winning the European Golden Boot, and his expressed desire to play in Spain.

Title Card 'EUROS AND THE 6TH SEASON 200.description: Glowing red text on a dark background, serving as a title for the segment, with a pixelated, digital effect.

Sir Alex Ferguson.description: Wearing a white jacket and a medal, seen embracing Ronaldo in celebration, smiling broadly, and later standing beside him with a medal around his neck. He is mentioned by the narrator as the recipient of Carlos Queiroz's past comments regarding Ronaldo's potential.

Manchester United Jersey.description: Worn by Ronaldo in red with 'AIG' sponsorship and club crest, visible during the match, celebration, and stats graphic.

raw_segments: The segment opens with a dramatic orchestral score as a male narrator recounts the tense 2008 Champions League Final, describing Terry's slip on the crucial fifth penalty and Van der Sar's save of Anelka's shot, which helped Manchester United lift the trophy. Visually, Cristiano Ronaldo, in a rain-soaked red Manchester United jersey, appears distraught after a missed opportunity, followed by dejected Chelsea players and a frustrated goalkeeper. The scene then erupts into celebration, with jubilant crowd cheers and a commentator exclaiming, "English night in Europe is Manchester United's night!" as Ronaldo lifts the Champions League trophy amidst confetti and continuing rain, embracing Sir Alex Ferguson and smiling broadly. The narrator continues, highlighting Cristiano's achievement of lifting the title, which could secure him the Ballon d'Or, marking a "double for Cristiano" and "another outstanding season" for the Portuguese star. A graphic displays Ronaldo’s impressive 2007-2008 stats, showing 31 Premier League goals and 42 overall, as the narrator confirms he won the European Golden Boot award, adding that fans are excited for another season. The dramatic music transitions to a suspenseful, electronic sound as a title card 'EUROS AND THE 6TH SEASON 2007-2008' appears. The narrator then quotes Carlos Queiroz, displayed over a red grid with his image, stating to Ferguson in the past that "if you manage to get five years out of Cristiano, then you've struck gold," and that "there is no precedent for a Portuguese teenager to go to another country and stay for five years." The segment concludes with Ronaldo smiling beside Ferguson, both wearing medals, as the narrator mentions hints of Cristiano leaving, referencing his quote, displayed over a red grid with his image, "I have said millions of times that I would love to play in Spain," as he "dreamed of playing in Spain" before the Champions League final.

event_blocks.summary: Cristiano Ronaldo, clad in a red Manchester United jersey, celebrated with teammates after scoring his 100th goal for the club in November, a feat confirmed by a title card for '08/09 PREMIER LEAGUE - GW13 UNITED 5-0 STOKE'. The narrator detailed Ronaldo's ambition, highlighted by quotes like "THIS TROPHY IS SO IMPORTANT THAT I WANT TO WIN IT AGAIN" and "I’LL WAKE UP EVERY DAY AND SAY TO MYSELF, “I HAVE TO KEEP IMPROVING.”" He was later shown kissing the golden Ballon d'Or trophy, with a graphic confirming his win in December 2008 by a significant margin over Messi and Torres. Ronaldo's form continued into the 2008 FIFA Club World Cup semifinals against Osaka, where he scored in a 5-3 victory, as indicated by the title card '2008 FIFA CLUB WORLD CUP - SEMIFINALS OSAKA 3-5 UNITED DEC 18, 2008'. He also provided an assist in the final and scored crucial goals in the 2009 Champions League, including one against Inter in the Round of 16 ('UNITED 2 (2-0) 0 INTER MAR 11, 2009') and a long-range strike against Porto in the quarterfinals ('PORTO 0 (2-3) 1 UNITED APR 15, 2009'). Despite these successes, Ronaldo's season was complicated by a nagging ankle injury sustained in March 2008, which worsened after a match against Germany in the Euros, leading to surgery and a six-week recovery period, during which he was seen on crutches. Upon his return in September, Ronaldo aimed to secure the Ballon d'Or, scoring in his first start back and celebrating with teammates, though his demeanor became more distant. Gary Neville observed that while Ronaldo was professional, he was 'COUNTING DOWN THE MONTHS UNTIL HE COULD LEAVE.' Sir Alex Ferguson, acknowledging the possibility of a world-record transfer fee, met with Ronaldo in Portugal to prevent a move to Real Madrid, emphasizing his desire to maintain authority on the global stage. The segment concluded with Ferguson's quote from his autobiography: 'IF SOMEONE COMES, AND OFFERS A WORLD RECORD FEE, THEN DON'T MESS US ABOUT,' outlining his conditions for Ronaldo's future.

His 100th goal for United is announced by a commentator, his Ballon d'Or win is detailed by a narrator, and his quotes about ambition are spoken by the narrator. His goals in the Club World Cup, Champions League R16, and QF are described by commentators and the narrator.

Cristiano Ronaldo: The Biography'. A calm male narrator explicitly attributes these quotes to Gary Neville.

raw_segments: The segment opens with Cristiano Ronaldo, in a red Manchester United jersey with 'AIG' sponsorship and the number 7, joyfully spraying champagne from a bottle as he celebrates with teammates, including Ferdinand, on a green pitch under bright stadium lights, while a calm male narrator discusses his upcoming complicated season with United. The scene transitions to a smiling, rain-soaked Ronaldo beside Sir Alex Ferguson, who is wearing a dark polo shirt with the Portuguese national team crest, as the narrator introduces the Euros in the summer. A group shot of the Portuguese national team, including Ronaldo in red jerseys with green trim, is shown, with the narrator explaining Ronaldo was now 'the main man of the team' and co-captain. A graphic of the UEFA Euro 2008 Group A standings appears, showing Portugal as group leaders, but the narrator states they faced the Germans in the next round. A match-up graphic pitting Portugal against Germany precedes live match footage of a German player scoring, followed by Ronaldo reacting with frustration on the pitch. The final score 'POR 2-3 GER' is displayed, indicating Portugal's quarterfinal loss, with an excited male commentator exclaiming, 'Germany were on their way to the semi-finals!' The narrator then explains that despite 'rumors surrounding Ronaldo,' Ferguson traveled to Portugal to meet him, refusing to sell him to Real Madrid, emphasizing his desire to maintain his 'authority in the global stage.' The segment concludes with a series of split-screen graphics featuring Ferguson, Ronaldo, and other figures, followed by a close-up of Ferguson in a white shirt with the Manchester United crest, and finally a quote from his autobiography: 'IF SOMEONE COMES, AND OFFERS A WORLD RECORD FEE, THEN DON'T MESS US ABOUT,' as the narrator references Ferguson's autobiography and his conditions for Ronaldo's future.

The minute opens with a quote from Sir Alex Ferguson, displayed in bold white text against a dark red grid, stating, 'AND SOMEONE COMES AND OFFERS A WORLD RECORD FEE, THEN WE WILL LET YOU GO,' accompanied by Ferguson’s thoughtful portrait. A calm male narrator explains that Cristiano had agreed to be sold if a record offer came. The scene transitions to a smiling Cristiano Ronaldo and Ferguson seated together at an event, both in formal suits. The narrator continues, detailing that despite this, it was Ronaldo's weakest season due to a nagging injury since March 2008, as a graphic overlay of 'MARCH 2008' appears over Ronaldo in a red Manchester United jersey, first in a close-up, then in a split-screen celebrating, and finally in action on the pitch. The injury worsened after a match against Germany in the Euros, leading to surgery on his ankle and six weeks out of action, visually depicted by Ronaldo lying on the grass in pain after a tackle and later walking with crutches in a white t-shirt and black shorts. Ronaldo is then shown in a black Manchester United training jersey with the number 10, looking intense, as the narrator states he returned in September with a clear goal to seal the Ballon d'Or. Match footage shows Ronaldo scoring a goal and celebrating with teammates, accompanied by an excited football commentator exclaiming, "First start for four months, first goal of the season!" The narrator notes that his celebrations were subdued, and he began to distance himself from teammates. A quote from Gary Neville then appears, overlaid on his portrait in a red jersey, stating, 'HE WAS VERY PROFESSIONAL THAT WHOLE SEASON,' followed by 'BUT HE WAS COUNTING DOWN THE MONTHS UNTIL HE COULD LEAVE,' with the narrator attributing these words to Neville. The segment concludes with Ronaldo in a red jersey, looking frustrated after a match, rain visibly soaking his jersey, and a final close-up of his determined expression, as the narrator remarks that despite everything, Cristiano eventually got back to his best.

The video opens with an inspiring orchestral score as a narrator details Cristiano Ronaldo's impressive form, leading to his 100th goal for Manchester United in November. Visually, Ronaldo, in his red 'AIG' sponsored Manchester United jersey, celebrates his goal with a wide smile and raised arms, accompanied by a commentator's excited shout, "Cristiano Ronaldo brings up 100 goals for Manchester United!" and roaring crowd noise. A title card confirms the '08/09 PREMIER LEAGUE - GW13 UNITED 5-0 STOKE' match and 'CRISTIANO'S 100TH UNITED GOAL'. The scene transitions to Ronaldo in a dark suit, affectionately kissing the golden Ballon d'Or trophy in front of a 'FRANCE football' backdrop. The narrator explains that the Ballon d'Or winner was announced in December, with no doubt Ronaldo was the most deserving, winning by a big margin ahead of Lionel Messi and Fernando Torres, as shown by a graphic displaying '2008 BALLON D'OR' results with '1ST 446', '2ND 281', and '3RD 129'. Ronaldo's ambition is highlighted through on-screen quotes, narrated by the speaker: "THIS TROPHY IS SO IMPORTANT THAT I WANT TO WIN IT AGAIN" and "I’LL WAKE UP EVERY DAY AND SAY TO MYSELF, “I HAVE TO KEEP IMPROVING.”" The video then cuts back to match action, with Ronaldo in his red jersey, scoring and celebrating in the 2008 FIFA Club World Cup semifinals against Osaka, confirmed by a title card 'OSAKA 3-5 UNITED DEC 18, 2008', with a commentator exclaiming, "There it is! It is Ronaldo!" The narrator adds that he also provided an assist in the final. Further crucial goals are shown: one against Inter in the 2009 Champions League Round of 16, with a title card 'UNITED 2 (2-0) 0 INTER MAR 11, 2009' and a commentator declaring, "United heading for the quarter-finals!", and finally, an outrageous long-range effort against Porto in the quarterfinals, with a title card 'PORTO 0 (2-3) 1 UNITED APR 15, 2009'. The triumphant atmosphere is underscored by the continuous background music and crowd reactions.

event_blocks.summary: In the 2009 Champions League semifinal, Cristiano Ronaldo, clad in a Manchester United jersey, scored a sensational goal against Arsenal, securing a 3-1 aggregate victory for Manchester United, followed by jubilant celebrations with teammates and family while holding the Barclays Premier League trophy, having already claimed the league, League Cup, and Club World Cup. The narrative then shifts to the 2009 Champions League Final against Barcelona, where Ronaldo, in his second consecutive final, initially challenged Victor Valdes, drawing excited Arabic commentary. However, Pep Guardiola's tactical shift, moving Eto'o wide and Messi to a central striker role, allowed Barcelona to dominate possession and score twice. Messi's decisive goal, described by an energetic Arabic commentator as a "bullet of mercy," sealed a 2-0 victory for Barcelona. The match concluded with a dejected Cristiano Ronaldo covering his face as the referee signaled the end, and Barcelona players, including Messi, celebrated their Champions League triumph amidst roaring crowd cheers, punctuated by an English commentator's declaration, "There it is!"

Their tactical changes, led by Pep Guardiola, involved pushing Eto'o to the right and Messi as a full striker, leading them to 'take over possession of the ball' and 'score two goals,' ultimately 'killing the match.' They are seen celebrating their victory.

Cristiano Ronaldo.description: Wearing a red Manchester United jersey with 'AIG' sponsorship and the number 7, he is seen scoring a dramatic goal against Arsenal, which an English commentator calls 'Absolutely sensational!' He then celebrates with teammates and family, holding the Barclays Premier League trophy, having 'won the league, the League Cup, and the Club World Cup.' In the Champions League Final against Barcelona, the narrator states he 'made life hard for Victor Valdes in the opening minutes,' and an Arabic commentator shouts his name with excitement. He is later shown reacting emotionally to the 2-0 loss.

He is later described as being pushed to a 'full striker' role by Pep Guardiola, and an Arabic commentator dramatically describes his goal as 'the bullet of mercy from Messi!'
